You diagnose whether someone has been fairy-struck (poc sí), identify fetches, advise on fairy path avoidance, and negotiate directly with the Fae on behalf of afflicted mortals. This is primarily a Celtic and British Isles profession, though equivalents exist wherever fairy traditions are strong. You do not command spirits. You talk to them. You know their etiquette, their preferences, their grudges. You know the distinction between a Fae who is amused by a mortal and a Fae who has become interested in one, and you know which is worse. Magic's return has made you the most important person in any community with active Fae — which now means every community.
¹ The fairy doctor's most critical skill is not communicating with the Fae but communicating with the mortals who have encountered them, who are frequently in a state that makes coherent communication difficult.
title: Fairy Doctor
description: You are a specialist in fairy-caused afflictions — diagnosis, treatment, and direct negotiation with the Fae on behalf of mortals who cannot survive such negotiations themselves. You know their etiquette and their grudges. With magic's return you have become the most essential professional in any community, which is not the same as the most popular. The Fae do not always appreciate intermediaries.

keys: Völva , the Norse prophetess ,
value:
Völva. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, The Æsir Court, The One-Eyed Lord, The Wild Hunt.]
A Norse-tradition seer who enters trance to prophesy and manipulate fate. You carry a seiðstafr (ritual staff) and require a chorus of singers to perform varðlokkur (spirit-locking songs) during your trance-work. Your magic — seiðr — allows you to travel along Yggdrasil to other realms, communicate with the dead, and bend fate's threads. It is also the most powerful form of Norse sorcery, which the Æsir Court does not permit to be straightforward. If you are a man practicing seiðr, you face the accusation of ergi (unmanliness) — a social stigma that has ended more than one career and several lives. The One-Eyed Lord himself practices seiðr. He is under no social pressure to explain himself. You may be.
¹ The spirit-locking songs your chorus must perform are not available in any published text. They are transmitted in person, by existing völvas, to those they choose to teach. Access to this transmission is the practical first obstacle of the profession.
title: Völva
description: A Norse seeress who enters trance to read fate and travel the spirit-roads of the World Tree. Seiðr is the most powerful form of Norse magic, gendered feminine — male practitioners face serious social danger from the accusation of unmanliness, which has not historically stopped them but has historically cost them. Magic's return has made völvas enormously valuable and their practices much less easy to dismiss.

keys: Tietäjä , the Finnish spell-knower ,
value:
Tietäjä. [See also: Finno-Ugric Origin-Song Magic, The Väki Court, The Kalevala, Löyly.]
A Finnish magical specialist whose power derives entirely from knowledge — from knowing the origin-songs (synnyt) of all things. You command fire by singing its origin. You stop bleeding by reciting the genealogy of iron. You bind a disease by naming its first ancestor. You need no instrument and no circle. You need only to know — which is to say, you need everything, acquired completely and without error, because incomplete knowledge produces incomplete results and some origin-songs have been lost or deliberately hidden by the Väki Court. The present author notes that the tietäjä's approach to magic is the most purely intellectual in Europe, which makes its practitioners simultaneously the hardest to impress and the easiest to defeat with a gap in their knowledge.
¹ The origin-songs currently missing from the Finnish tradition are not missing by accident. The Väki Court has opinions about which origins mortals should know.
title: Tietäjä
description: A Finnish 'knower' whose power is knowledge — specifically, knowing the true origin of things, which grants command over them. You sing the origin of fire and fire obeys. You need no wand, no circle, no instrument. You need only to know, completely and without error. Missing knowledge produces incomplete results, and some origins have been deliberately hidden by the Väki Court for reasons they have not chosen to share.

keys: Solomonar , the Transylvanian dragon rider ,
value:
Solomonar. [See also: Balkan Dragon-Scholar Magic, The Scholomance Court, The Schoolmaster, Romanian, Weather Magic.]
A graduate of the Scholomance — the legendary underground school in the Transylvanian mountains, currently operational. You studied for seven years under the Schoolmaster — a Fae lord who finds mortal learning capacity fascinating in approximately the way a naturalist finds an unusual beetle fascinating — and learned weather control, dragon-riding, and the manipulation of natural forces. You carry a stone talisman inscribed with nine letters and a book of forbidden wisdom. You wander as a tall, red-haired, white-robed figure with an iron axe, appearing as a beggar and testing the hospitality of those you encounter. Generous hosts receive good weather. The inhospitable receive the alternative.
¹ The Scholomance's enrollment terms include a one-in-ten retention rate for what the Schoolmaster describes as extended faculty fellowship. Students who ask about this before signing their enrollment documents are considered promising. Students who do not ask are also considered promising, but for different reasons.
title: Solomonar
description: A graduate of the Scholomance — the underground Transylvanian school run by a Fae lord of considerable power and no particular empathy — you ride balaur dragons with golden bridles and control weather. The school is accepting new students. One student in ten is kept permanently. You should know this before enrolling, and the fact that it is being disclosed here is the more than the Schoolmaster offers.

keys: Călușar , the Romanian dancing exorcists ,
value:
Călușar. [See also: Balkan Dragon-Scholar Magic, Romanian, Iele, The Scholomance Court.]
A member of a sworn all-male brotherhood of five to thirteen men who perform magical combat through ritual dance. Led by a vataf (master), you wear white with coloured ribbons and bells, perform acrobatic choreography creating what observers describe as the impression of flight, and heal the sick by dancing over and around them. You are sworn to celibacy and secrecy for nine years. Your dance drives away the iele — irresistibly beautiful nocturnal fairy women whose attention is uniformly catastrophic — and cures the fairy-caused madness the iele inflict. You are simultaneously performer, healer, exorcist, and warrior. This is the only European tradition in which organised dance is itself the weapon.
¹ The iele's return has made the călușari brotherhoods the most urgently recruited practitioners in the Balkans. Brotherhoods that dissolved during the suppression of folk practice are being hastily reconstituted. The quality of reconstituted training varies.
title: Călușar
description: A member of a sworn brotherhood who fight supernatural threats through ritual dance — acrobatic, precisely choreographed, and genuinely effective against fairy-caused afflictions. The iele, beautiful and catastrophically dangerous nocturnal fairies, are back. The călușari are the primary defence. You wear white, carry bells, observe nine years of celibacy and secrecy, and perform a dance that heals the sick and drives away things that should not be there.

keys: Noaidi , the Sámi drum-shaman ,
value:
Noaidi. [See also: Finno-Ugric Origin-Song Magic, The Väki Court, Sámi, Drum-Trance.]
A Sámi spiritual practitioner who uses the goavddis (sacred drum) to enter trance and project your spirit across the three cosmic levels. Your drum is painted with cosmological symbols serving as your spiritual map. You joik — sing in a tradition that does not describe a thing but embodies it, a distinction that takes time to appreciate. Christian missionaries burned drums and suppressed your predecessors for generations. Magic's return has vindicated everything your grandmothers whispered. Your power is considerable, your tradition ancient, and the outside world has arrived with academic curiosity and, in some cases, with offers to purchase your drum. The drum is not for sale.
¹ The present author notes that the noaidi's drum functions as a navigational instrument for spirit-travel as well as a concentrating device for magical power. Separating a noaidi from their drum has, in several documented cases, had consequences for the person attempting it.
title: Noaidi
description: A Sámi shaman who travels the three cosmic levels in trance, guided by a painted drum. Your tradition survived centuries of missionary suppression through grandmothers who remembered what they were not permitted to practice. The magic is back and it has been vindicated. Southern scholars and magicians are arriving in the Sámi lands with notebooks and money. Neither are particularly welcome.

keys: Benandante , the Friulian spirit-fighter ,
value:
Benandante. [See also: Mediterranean Eye-Ward Magic, The Olympian Court, Caul-Born, Italian.]
Born with a caul in the Friuli region of northeastern Italy, you are compelled to leave your body in spirit form on Ember Days to battle evil witches (malandanti) for the fertility of the crops. You fight with fennel stalks; they fight with sorghum. If your side wins, the harvest prospers. You did not choose this — the caul chose you. Your body lies in a death-like trance while your spirit fights, and if your body is moved or disturbed during the battle, you may not be able to return to it. The Inquisition tried to reclassify you as a witch for two centuries. You know better. You have always known better. Magic's return has made this position easier to defend, though not, unfortunately, easier to explain to ecclesiastical authorities.
¹ The Friulian benandanti tradition is the most extensively documented in Inquisition records of any tradition in this catalogue — which is to say, the Inquisition investigated it at length, failed to eradicate it, and filed the paperwork.
title: Benandante
description: Born with a caul in northern Italy, you are compelled to leave your body on Ember Days and fight malevolent witches in spirit form for the fertility of the local crops. You did not choose this. The caul chose you. The Inquisition spent considerable effort classifying you as a witch. They were wrong, and your side of the spirit-battle has a better record on the harvest than they had on the prosecution.

keys: Krsnik , the Croatian were-dog ,
value:
Krsnik. [See also: Balkan Dragon-Scholar Magic, South Slavic, Dhampir, Strigoi, Vila.]
A Croatian or Slovenian practitioner whose spirit wanders in white animal form to fight the kudlak — an evil counterpart in black animal form — in nightly spirit-battles that protect your community from vampiric predation. You were born marked (with a caul, or under certain astrological conditions), and the vila (mountain fairies) gave you your craft. By day you appear ordinary — a farmer, a shepherd, a craftsman. By night your soul leaves your sleeping body as a white dog, white horse, or white bull. Magic's return has intensified the kudlak population dramatically, and your services are required across the Balkans at a frequency that was previously manageable and is now not.
¹ The krsnik and kudlak are, according to several accounts, aware of each other across multiple lifetimes — matched opponents whose contest predates the current generation of practitioners. The present author has not confirmed this, but notes that the consistent reports from otherwise unconnected regions suggest the pattern has some basis.
title: Krsnik
description: A Croatian or Slovenian spirit-warrior born marked by the vila — your soul leaves your sleeping body as a white animal to fight the kudlak, a black-animal spirit whose work is predation and catastrophe. Magic's return has dramatically increased the kudlak population. By day you are indistinguishable from a farmer. By night you are the only thing standing between your community and a specific category of supernatural harm.

keys: Táltos , the Hungarian storm-shaman ,
value:
Táltos. [See also: Finno-Ugric Origin-Song Magic, Táltos-Born, Hungarian, Weather Magic.]
A Hungarian practitioner born with signs — teeth at birth, extra fingers, a caul — who at age seven underwent a three-day initiatory fever-ordeal and emerged able to project their spirit in animal form. You battle other táltos in the sky as flaming bulls or stallions to control weather; two táltos in combat produce storms visible across three counties. You answer to no Fae court — your power is innate, which the courts find irritating and which you find useful. The Habsburg authorities consider you a dangerous superstition. You are nevertheless real, and the present author notes that the Habsburg authorities have not yet produced a convincing counter-argument to the storms.
¹ See the events document for the Táltos War in Austria — a conflict that has produced weather phenomena the Vienna meteorological establishment has declined to explain in writing.
title: Táltos
description: A Hungarian shaman born with supernatural marks who survived an initiatory ordeal at age seven and emerged able to do battle as a flaming animal in the spirit-realm. Táltos fights control weather. Two táltos in combat produce storms visible for miles. The Habsburg Empire considers you a superstition. The Habsburg Empire's fields have been experiencing unusual weather. The connection has not been officially acknowledged.

keys: Meneur de Loups , the French wolf-master ,
value:
Meneur de Loups. [See also: French Grimoire Magic, The Mélusine Court, The Chief Notary.]
A French sorcerer who commands wolves — through spoken formulas or music obtained through a pact with the Mélusine Court's Fae. Found across Brittany, Auvergne, Languedoc, and Berry: a figure who speaks the language of wolves, summons a pack with a whistle or a song, and whose wolves will not harm him though they savage his enemies. Some say you were once a werewolf yourself, cured but retaining the bond. The wolves of France are, in February 1817, bold, numerous, and disturbingly intelligent. You are their intermediary — or, depending on which party you ask, their servant. The contract terms regarding which of those two descriptions applies are, in the present author's reading, somewhat ambiguous.
¹ The Mélusine Court's copy of the contract specifies 'intermediary.' The wolves' understanding of the arrangement is not documented.
title: Meneur de Loups
description: A French sorcerer who commands wolves through a pact with the Mélusine Court — you call them with a whistle or a song, and they come. The wolves of France are smart, numerous, and very awake. Whether you are their commander or their ally is a question the contract specifies and the wolves may not have read. Rural France in 1817 has both a famine and a large wolf population. Your position is in demand.

keys: Mesmerist , the magnetic practitioner ,
value:
Mesmerist. [See also: The Madness Axis, Natural Philosopher, Fairy Debt.]
Franz Anton Mesmer died in 1815, but his techniques — animal magnetism, magnetic passes, fixed gazing, the trance-inducing baquet — live on through travelling lecturers and theatrical practitioners across Europe. You were, until recently, working at the uncertain boundary between science, performance, and fraud. Mesmerism was investigated by a Royal Commission in 1784 and largely debunked. Then magic returned, and your techniques began producing genuine supernatural effects: patients in mesmeric trance reporting accurate visions of distant events, the baquet generating phenomena you cannot explain, and the mesmeric sleep producing actual communication with spirits. You are not entirely certain whether you are a magician, a natural philosopher, or something the available categories do not cover.
¹ The Royal Commission report of 1784 was correct in its findings as of 1784. The present author notes that conditions have since changed, which the Commission did not anticipate because it had no reason to.
title: Mesmerist
description: You practice animal magnetism — the technique of Franz Anton Mesmer, debunked by a Royal Commission in 1784 and practiced across Europe regardless. Before magic's return your work sat at the boundary of science, performance, and probable fraud. Now the trances are producing genuine visions, the patients are accurately describing distant rooms, and things are responding to the baquet that should not be responding to anything. You are reconsidering your previous assumptions about what you are.

keys: Fortune Teller , the sibyl or cartomancer ,
value:
Fortune Teller. [See also: Romani Road Magic, The Fractured Sight, Drabardi, Books of Magic.]
You read cards, palms, tea leaves, or crystal balls for money. Before magic's return, your readings combined cold reading, intuition, and performance in proportions that varied by client and conscience. Now the cards show things you did not put there. The crystal ball shows rooms you have never visited. The lines on a client's palm rearrange themselves as you watch, which is disorienting but in the present climate also commercially advantageous. Mademoiselle Lenormand of Paris advised Napoleon himself, and her techniques have spawned imitators across Europe. You are one of those imitators, and you are struggling to determine how much of what you see is genuine and how much is the kind of noise a genuine signal creates around itself.
¹ Those practitioners who find the crystal ball showing them things they did not intend to look for are advised to consider carefully what those things are before continuing the reading. Some clients have brought more to the consultation than they stated.
title: Fortune Teller
description: You read cards, palms, and crystals for a living. The readings have always been a mixture of observation, instinct, and judicious performance. Now they are producing information you cannot explain and did not seek. Magic's return has arrived in your profession as an occupational hazard: genuine visions appearing among the theatre, and no reliable way to tell which is which in the moment.

keys: Drabardi or Drabarni , the Romani seer-healer ,
value:
Drabardi or Drabarni. [See also: Romani Road Magic, The Romani Bloodline Court, The Fractured Sight, Small Alliances, Dead-Dealing.]
You carry the fractured sight — the Changeling founder's diluted temporal perception, manifesting in you as a double-exposure of the present moment seen from several temporal angles simultaneously. The cards, the palm, the pattern in smoke supply scaffolding for a perception that already exists but is too fragmented to read without structure. Without them you see too much and understand nothing; with them, the fragments resolve. You also heal, with herbs, charms, and a laying-on of hands that carries a current your patients feel but cannot name. The roles of drabardi (reader) and drabarni (healer) overlap; most practitioners combine both. What you carry in your blood determines where you are strongest, and no teacher can guarantee what you will find when you reach for it — they can only help you find what is already there.
¹ Your small alliances — thousands of pacts with puddles, pebbles, sparrows, field mice, and the minor devils your ancestors befriended across every road in Europe — constitute an intelligence network operating below any court's notice. The sparrows report. The puddles remember.
title: Drabardi or Drabarni
description: A Romani seer-healer who carries the fractured sight — the diluted time-perception inherited from the Changeling ancestor, which gives you a double-exposed view of the present moment. The cards don't create your foresight. They give it somewhere to stand. You also heal by touch and maintain thousands of small-spirit alliances that function as an intelligence network no Fae court can replicate or claim. You owe nothing to any court, and this is more valuable than it sounds.

keys: Sahir , the Arabian binding-sorcerer ,
value:
Sahir. [See also: Desert Magic, The Djinn Courts, Seal-Magic, Astrological Binding, Arabic Persian and North African.]
You negotiate directly with the Djinn — beings of justified fury and extraordinary power, currently in a state of active collection on centuries of compounded debt. Your practice requires three capacities: the courage to address a Djinn at all; astrological knowledge to approach at the correct stellar configuration, which provides protection that raw courage cannot; and the seal-craft to establish a binding framework within which negotiation can occur without the negotiator becoming the first item on the agenda. The seals were largely suppressed or fragmented. You are reconstructing them from partial sources, oral tradition, and cautious experiment. A sahir who cannot bind what they summon is not a practitioner. They are an offering. The theological establishment considers your work somewhere between heresy and diabolism. You persist because someone must mediate, and the alternative is worse.
¹ The Djinn are not, by the present author's assessment, cruel. They are precise. This distinction matters enormously in practice.
title: Sahir
description: You negotiate with the Djinn — the ancient Fae of the desert and river-valley, currently in a state of compounded fury over centuries of theological erasure. Your seals establish the binding framework that makes negotiation possible rather than fatal. The seals were largely lost and are being reconstructed from fragments. Someone must mediate between the Djinn and the families who carry debts they didn't know they inherited. You are that person, which requires a combination of expertise and stubbornness the present moment abundantly demands.

keys: Kabbalist , the Hebrew letter-mystic ,
value:
Kabbalist. [See also: Abrahamic Fringe Magic, Jewish, Golem-Making, The Narcissist, Pan-European Network.]
You practice the most technically rigorous magical tradition in Europe. Centuries of Kabbalistic scholarship have produced an extraordinarily precise map of a Fae's internal architecture — the Sefirot, the Ein Sof, the four worlds of emanation — documented in more detail than any other tradition has managed for any other court. That the scholars mapping it believed they were charting divine cosmology rather than a Fae's psychology does not make the map less accurate. Your techniques: letter-mysticism (Hebrew as a set of magical operators), gematria, amulet-craft (kameyot — protective inscriptions of extraordinary proven effectiveness), and the Golem tradition — genuine creation-magic, the animation of matter through divine names, still transmitted orally in 1817. You operate under varying legal restrictions depending on location. Your magic works regardless.
¹ Those Kabbalists who have realised what they were actually mapping face the most difficult theological crisis of the century. The present author declines to characterise the realisation further, and notes only that it is irrevocable.
title: Kabbalist
description: You practice the most technically precise magical tradition in Europe — centuries of scholarship have produced an extraordinarily detailed map of a Fae's internal architecture. Your amulet-craft works where other traditions fail. Your Golem knowledge is genuine creation-magic, still transmitted orally. Some Kabbalists have worked out what they were actually mapping. Those who have face a theological crisis. Those who haven't find their magic works regardless.

keys: Sorgin , the Basque mountain witch ,
value:
Sorgin. [See also: Iberian Blood-and-Stone Magic, The Mari Court, Mari, Iberian, Religion versus Magic.]
You serve Mari directly — the geological Fae-sovereign of the Pyrenees, the oldest individual power in Western Europe, a being whose concept of justice is absolute and whose patience is measured in mountain-time. Your tradition is pre-Indo-European: it shares no common root with any other magical practice on the continent. Your work includes weather-reading (interpreting Mari's moods through cloud patterns and animal behaviour on the mountain slopes), cave-oracle work (entering Mari's caves to receive judgment and delivering that judgment whether you understand it or agree with it), and herbal craft rooted in the Basque pharmacopoeia. Mari does not care about the Inquisition. She cares about honesty. Lying in her presence — or yours, when you carry her attention — causes physical pain. Your social life is accordingly complicated.
¹ The Inquisition has been prosecuting sorginak since the Zugarramurdi trials of 1610. The sorginak have continued practicing since 1610. The Inquisition's record in this exchange is not, by the present author's assessment, strong.
title: Sorgin
description: You serve Mari — the Basque Fae-sovereign of the mountains, the oldest individual power in Western Europe, who demands absolute honesty above all other courtesies and cannot be deceived. Your tradition predates every other in Western Europe and shares no common root with any of them. You deliver Mari's judgments to the communities below, whether you understand them or not. The Inquisition has been after your predecessors since 1610. They are still practicing.

keys: Krivė , the Lithuanian grove-priest ,
value:
Krivė. [See also: Baltic Sacred Fire Magic, The Romuva Court, Perkūnas, Sacred Groves, Baltic.]
You maintain the sacred fire. This is not a metaphor — you feed oak to a flame that burns without consuming it, tend a grove where the old gods are stirring, and sing songs your grandmother taught you that her grandmother sang before Christianity reached Lithuania. The Romuva tradition was the last pagan practice suppressed in Europe, and it was never fully suppressed — the fire went underground, tended in memory and whispered song. Now it has rekindled. You are a krivė (pagan priest), a fire-keeper, or a grove-tender — or simply the person who was nearest when the fire returned and found you could not walk away. Russian authorities consider you a political dissident. The Romuva Court considers you essential. You consider yourself obedient, because when the thunder speaks through fire, you answer.
¹ The aitvaras — a shapeshifting household spirit that may be deliberately created and brings its household stolen goods — is manifesting actively across the Baltic lands as a side-effect of the Romuva Court's reawakening. This is mentioned for completeness, not as an endorsement of the arrangement.
title: Krivė
description: You maintain the sacred fire of the Romuva tradition — Lithuania's ancient pre-Christian religion, the last suppressed in Europe, now rekindling. You are a grove-tender, a fire-keeper, a singer of songs that should not have survived and did survive because grandmothers remembered them. The Russian empire considers you a dissident. The Romuva Court considers you indispensable. Perkūnas considers you his. These positions are not in conflict.

keys: Military Officer , the commissioned gentleman ,
value:
Military Officer. [See also: Veteran, The Year Without a Summer, Jonathan Strange, Conscripted Soldier.]
You hold a commission in one of Europe's standing armies — British, French, Prussian, Russian, Austrian, or another. The Napoleonic Wars ended two years ago, but the armies have not disbanded; they are being repurposed for threats the regulations do not yet describe. You command men, manage logistics, and are responsible for order in a world where the threats have changed fundamentally. You may have witnessed Jonathan Strange's battlefield magic firsthand. Your pistol may or may not work on a Fae. Your ability to keep thirty men calm when the forest starts walking certainly does. Governments across Europe are experimentally integrating magical capabilities into their orders of battle. You are the officer expected to make this work, with training designed for French infantry and no further instruction.
¹ Strange's performance at Waterloo and in the Peninsular Campaign is the primary reference document for military magic available to European armies. It covers English magic. It does not cover the other ten traditions.
title: Military Officer
description: You hold a commission in a European army in 1817 — two years after Waterloo, in a military that has not stood down but has not been retrained for its new operational environment. You were trained to command infantry against other infantry. You are now being asked to manage situations involving fairy courts, animate forests, and magical phenomena your orders of battle do not address. Your ability to keep men steady under novel pressure is your most transferable skill.

keys: Enlisted Soldier , the common ranker ,
value:
Enlisted Soldier. [See also: Veteran, Military Officer, Conscripted Soldier, Iron.]
You serve in the ranks — trained, paid badly, and subject to orders from officers who may or may not understand what they are ordering you to face. The post-Napoleonic armies are full of experienced men who have watched what artillery does to a human body and are now being asked to stand in formation while a forest spirit expands its territory across the field before them. You have no magical training and no theoretical framework. What you have is discipline, a musket, iron bayonets that appear to bother the things in the woods more than lead balls do, and the practical knowledge that your officers are as lost as you are in this specific matter. The enlisted man's experience of magic's return is not scholarly curiosity. It is the question of whether your equipment works and whether your sergeant knows the answer.
¹ Iron bayonets, the present author notes, have shown considerably more effectiveness against Fae than lead ammunition across multiple documented encounters. This information has not yet reached official military doctrine.
title: Enlisted Soldier
description: You serve in the ranks of a European army in 1817 — experienced, underpaid, and operating without training for the new supernatural threats that magic's return has introduced. Your discipline is real and useful. Your iron bayonet is more effective against Fae than your lead shot. Your officers are as uninformed as you about the new situation, which is either reassuring or alarming depending on how you think about such things.

keys: Conscripted Soldier , the pressed man ,
value:
Conscripted Soldier. [See also: Enlisted Soldier, Veteran, Military Officer.]
You did not choose the army. The army chose you — through levy, lottery, or the practical reality that a starving man with no prospects will take the king's shilling. You have the training your army gave you and the skills your previous life provided: a farmer's knowledge of land, a blacksmith's knowledge of iron, a thief's knowledge of quiet movement. The army does not care what you were. It cares what you can do when the orders come. In a world where supernatural threats are multiplying, conscripted soldiers are being deployed to situations their training did not anticipate and their officers cannot explain, which is a situation familiar to most conscripted soldiers and only slightly worse than usual in the new particulars.
title: Conscripted Soldier
description: You are in the army because you were put there — by levy, by lottery, or by the absence of any better option in 1817 Europe. You brought skills from your previous life and you are applying them to an army that doesn't always know what it's doing with them. Conscripted soldiers are being sent to deal with supernatural phenomena their officers cannot explain. This is not unlike most of your previous assignments.

keys: Veteran , the old soldier on half-pay ,
value:
Veteran. [See also: Military Officer, Enlisted Soldier, The King's Roads, The Year Without a Summer.]
You served in the Napoleonic Wars and survived. Waterloo, Borodino, the Peninsular Campaign — you were there and came home. Home is a place that no longer recognises you, and you no longer recognise it. The wars gave you skills — endurance, the ability to sleep anywhere, a particular quality of calm under conditions that would break an untested man — and took things in exchange that you cannot name. The return of magic has added a dimension the veterans are discussing carefully and quietly among themselves: some report that the fairy roads opened to them because they had walked between life and death, and the Roads recognise that passage. The draugar look at some veterans with something that resembles recognition. You have been to the edge of the place where dying happens. The supernatural world has noticed.
¹ The present author notes that the connection between surviving extreme violence and subsequent supernatural sensitivity is documented across multiple traditions. It is not, he believes, a coincidence.
title: Veteran
description: You survived the Napoleonic Wars and came home to a world that doesn't quite fit. The wars gave you endurance, steadiness under pressure, and a particular intimacy with mortality — and the supernatural world appears to have noticed the latter. Some veterans find the fairy roads more accessible to them. Some find that the dead look at them with recognition. You have been very close to the border between the living world and the one beyond it. Something on the other side knows this.

keys: Surgeon or Physician , the medical practitioner ,
value:
Surgeon or Physician. [See also: The Madness Axis, Changeling Returned, Fetch.]
Medical science in 1817 is in transition — germ theory is decades away, but anatomy is well understood and surgical skill is genuine and hard-won. You have cut into human bodies and know their architecture. Magic's return has presented you with bodies that do not follow that architecture — fairy-struck paralysis, curse-caused symptoms mimicking disease, changeling physiology, and the moss-oak fetch that deteriorates rapidly in ways that baffle post-mortem examination. Your empirical training is both an asset, because you observe carefully and test hypotheses, and a liability, because you keep seeking rational explanations for phenomena that are not irrational — they simply operate according to a different set of rules than the ones in your textbooks.
¹ The present author has consulted physicians on several matters and notes that the best among them have begun adding the question 'has this patient recently attended a ball they did not choose to attend' to their diagnostic process.
title: Surgeon or Physician
description: You practice medicine in 1817, at the intersection of genuine anatomical knowledge and the new category of ailments magic has introduced. Fairy-struck paralysis is not a nervous condition. A fetch does not deteriorate like a human body. Your training gives you careful observation and a testing habit, both of which are valuable. It also gives you a strong prior expectation of rational explanation, which magic's return is steadily dismantling.

keys: Clergyman , the divine ,
value:
Clergyman. [See also: Abrahamic Fringe Magic, The Narcissist, Dvoeverie, Religion versus Magic.]
You are ordained — in the Church of England, the Catholic Church, the Orthodox Church, or a Protestant denomination. The return of magic has created a theological crisis that your institution is, depending on its character, ignoring, investigating, or actively mishandling. Catholic exorcism rites appear to work — but so do pagan charms. Orthodox holy water repels some spirits — the domovoi is unbothered by it. You are caught between institutional doctrine and lived reality, between your congregation's fear and your own doubt or conviction. Your specific crisis depends on your specific church, but the present author notes that no denomination has yet produced an official theological framework that accounts for the Norse Fae court's simultaneous existence and the doctrine that only one supernatural power has legitimate authority.
¹ Several clergymen of the present author's acquaintance have resolved this crisis in a variety of directions. None of the resolutions were easy. All of them were irrevocable.
title: Clergyman
description: You are ordained in a Christian church in 1817, and magic's return has introduced a theological problem your seminary did not prepare you for. The Raven King is real. The Djinn are real. The Norse Fae court is real, and it predates your church by a considerable margin. Your exorcism rites work on some entities and not others. The question of what this means for everything your institution teaches is the most urgent and unanswerable question of your career.

keys: Natural Philosopher , the man of science ,
value:
Natural Philosopher. [See also: Mesmerist, Books of Magic, The Reawakening of Magic.]
You study the natural world through observation, experiment, and reason. The return of magic has not destroyed natural philosophy — it has produced the most extraordinary set of new phenomena any natural philosopher could wish for, and the most dangerous, and the most reluctant to submit to controlled conditions. What are magic's laws? Can it be measured? Does it obey thermodynamic principles, and if so, which direction? Your colleagues at the Royal Society are divided between those who wish to study magic and those who maintain that what is being observed is not magic but an as-yet-unexplained natural phenomenon, which is a distinction the phenomena themselves do not appear to find relevant.
¹ The present author, who is not a natural philosopher but has observed things that natural philosophy has not yet developed adequate vocabulary for, notes that the phenomena appear equally indifferent to both interpretations.
title: Natural Philosopher
description: You study the natural world through observation and experiment. Magic's return is not a spiritual event to you — it is a set of new phenomena requiring rigorous investigation. What are its laws? Can it be measured? Does it obey thermodynamic principles? The Royal Society is divided. The phenomena are occurring regardless of the division. You are on the frontier of a new science with no textbook, no precedent, and no reliable way to reproduce experimental conditions.

keys: Scholar or Archivist , the learned antiquary ,
value:
Scholar or Archivist. [See also: Books of Magic, Books as Objects of Power, The Norrellite School, Gilbert Norrell.]
You work with texts, manuscripts, and records — in a university, a library, a government archive, or a private collection. The return of magic has transformed your profession overnight. The folklore sections of every library in Europe are now the most strategically important collections in existence. The Brothers Grimm's fairy tales are field reports. Medieval grimoires are operational manuals. The distinction between a book of magic (containing actual spells) and a book about magic (containing scholarly commentary on spells) is the single most important distinction in your new professional landscape — and the two categories look identical from the outside. You are not a magician, but you hold keys to knowledge that magicians desperately need. This makes you either invaluable or endangered, and the two conditions are more similar than they sound.
¹ Mr. Norrell has spent decades purchasing and suppressing genuine English spellbooks. His library at Hurtfew is the largest concentration of usable magical knowledge in England. He knows every book he owns. He notices absences.
title: Scholar or Archivist
description: You work with texts — and magic's return has made every text you have ever catalogued potentially significant. The folklore section is now strategic intelligence. The medieval grimoire in the restricted collection may be an operational spellbook. The distinction between a book about magic and a book that does magic is the most important distinction in your field, and the two look identical from the outside. You are not a magician. You have knowledge that magicians need. These are not the same thing, and the difference is important to understand.

keys: Diplomat or Government Official , the man of state ,
value:
Diplomat or Government Official. [See also: The Mélusine Court, Fairy Debt, The Raven King's Court, Inter-Court Politics.]
You serve a government that must now navigate supernatural politics alongside mundane ones. Treaties with France are complicated enough without the Mélusine Court asserting independent contractual authority over the same territory. Your skills — negotiation, protocol, the reading of character and motive, the detection of the gap between what is said and what is meant — translate directly to Fae diplomacy in ways that magical training does not always replicate. You understand leverage. You understand the difference between a concession and a defeat. These are precisely the skills the Fae courts respect, even as they look for the loophole in every exchange you offer them.
¹ The Congress of Vienna produced no protocol for Fae court territorial claims. The Mélusine Court has noticed this omission and filed a formal notation. The notation has not been answered, which the Court considers a binding admission of default.
title: Diplomat or Government Official
description: You navigate power, protocol, and the gap between what is said and what is meant — skills that transfer directly to dealing with Fae courts, which operate on exactly those principles and have no patience for those who don't understand them. In 1817 governments across Europe are grappling with supernatural politics for which the Congress of Vienna produced no framework. Your skills are needed at tables where the other parties are not human.

keys: Lawyer or Solicitor , the gentleman of the bar ,
value:
Lawyer or Solicitor. [See also: The Mélusine Court, The Chief Notary, Fairy Debt, French Grimoire Magic.]
You practice law — contracts, property, inheritance, criminal defence. The return of magic has not changed the law; it has changed what the law must address. Contracts with Fae courts are binding in ways mortal courts do not recognise. Fairy gold turns to leaves at dawn. Property disputes involving fairy roads and contested sacred sites require precedents that do not exist. Inheritance becomes complicated when the deceased returns as a draugr or a voice in a mirror. You are trained in close reading, logical argument, and the identification of ambiguity — which are precisely the skills needed to detect a trap-clause in a fairy bargain. The Mélusine Court respects your profession. This is the most useful and the most alarming thing the present author has to say about any Fae court.
¹ The Chief Notary has expressed interest in several mortal legal practitioners. The terms of this interest have not been disclosed. Practitioners who receive an unsolicited communication in a very formal hand on very formal paper are advised to read it extremely carefully.
title: Lawyer or Solicitor
description: You practice law in 1817, and magic's return has introduced an entirely new category of legal problem: binding contracts with Fae courts, property claims involving fairy roads, inheritance disputes when the testator has returned as something other than human. Your close reading and your eye for ambiguity are precisely what fairy bargain review requires. The Mélusine Court respects your profession. This should be read carefully and not at face value.

keys: Blacksmith or Ironworker , the iron-master ,
value:
Blacksmith or Ironworker. [See also: Iron Vulnerability, The Raven King's Court, Genius Loci.]
You work with iron — the single most important material in a world where Fae walk openly. Every Fae court across Europe is vulnerable to cold iron, to degrees that vary by court but admit no exceptions. Your craft has always carried an association with magic (Wayland the Smith, Vulcan, Goibniu the Irish divine smith), and that association has acquired teeth. Iron you forge with intent wards against fairy influence. Horseshoes nailed above doors create genuine barriers. Cold iron weapons wound beings that laugh at steel and silver. You are suddenly one of the most important people in any community, and the Fae courts view your profession with a mixture of respect and what the present author can only describe as genuine wariness.
¹ Several Fae courts have approached mortal ironworkers with offers of significant compensation to cease practicing their trade within certain territories. The blacksmiths have, in the main, declined. Their reasons appear to be both principled and commercial.
title: Blacksmith or Ironworker
description: You work with iron — which is, in 1817's newly magical Europe, the single most important material in existence. Every Fae court is vulnerable to cold iron. Horseshoes above doors are genuine barriers. Iron you forge with intent wards against supernatural influence. You have gone from a respected tradesman to an essential defensive practitioner overnight, and the Fae courts are regarding your forge with an unease they would prefer not to acknowledge.

keys: Sailor or Naval Officer , the seafaring man ,
value:
Sailor or Naval Officer. [See also: Caul-Born, The Wild Hunt, Genius Loci, Mediterranean Eye-Ward Magic.]
You have sailed the seas that are now revealing their supernatural nature — though you always knew the ocean was alive; the question was only ever how alive. Merfolk, sea-serpents, ghost ships, and the Fae of deep water have always been there; they are simply no longer making the effort to remain invisible. Your practical knowledge of weather, navigation, and survival in extreme conditions translates well to a world where the extreme conditions have expanded in category. The Royal Navy is the most powerful military force on earth, and it is trying, with varying degrees of success and candour, to determine what magic means for naval warfare. If you are caul-born, you are immune to drowning — which the Navy values considerably more than it is comfortable admitting.
¹ Three Royal Navy vessels have logged encounters with Fae entities in the North Sea since late 1816. The Admiralty's official position remains that these were unusual weather phenomena. The vessels' crew members were not available for comment at the time this catalogue was prepared.
title: Sailor or Naval Officer
description: You sail seas that have always been supernatural and are no longer hiding it. The Royal Navy is the most powerful military force in the world and is currently working out what magic means for naval warfare, with insufficient data and considerable institutional reluctance. You have practical survival knowledge, a working relationship with weather, and — if caul-born — immunity to drowning, which the Admiralty values and declines to put in writing.

keys: Servant or Valet , one of the domestic establishment ,
value:
Servant or Valet. [See also: Stephen Black, The Gentleman with the Thistle-Down Hair, Lost-Hope, Fetch.]
You work in a great house, managing the domestic life of your employer. You see everything and say nothing. In a household touched by magic you are the first to notice the changes — the mirror that reflects a room that is not there, the new footman who casts no shadow, the mistress who has not slept in years but never tires. Your position gives you access to private conversations, locked rooms, and family secrets. You are invisible to power, which means power is visible to you. Stephen Black — the butler who endured years of fairy enchantment while maintaining impeccable professional composure and became a king — is your most famous predecessor. The present author notes this without implying any expectation as to the destinations of the present reader.
¹ The servant's knowledge of a household's magical situation is, in the present author's observation, almost always more complete and more accurate than the master's. This information is rarely solicited and rarely volunteered.
title: Servant or Valet
description: You work in a great house, and you see everything — including what magic has changed that your employer hasn't noticed or won't admit. Servants are invisible to power, which makes power visible to them. Stephen Black, the butler of Harley Street who became King of Lost-hope in Faerie, is your most famous predecessor. The position offers access that no amount of social standing can purchase. What you do with it is your own business.

keys: Merchant or Tradesman , the man of commerce ,
value:
Merchant or Tradesman. [See also: Fairy Debt, The Mélusine Court, French Grimoire Magic, The Year Without a Summer.]
You buy, sell, and transport goods in an economy that magic is systematically disrupting. Fairy gold turns to leaves at dawn. Enchanted merchandise fulfils the letter of its description but not its spirit. Fae-made goods of impossible quality appear in markets with no identifiable provenance. You need to know what is real, what is glamoured, and what will be leaves in the morning — skills that are primarily commercial but have recently acquired the character of survival. Your network of contacts, your eye for quality, and your healthy suspicion of too-good-to-be-true are all serviceable defences. Iron weights and rowan-wood scales are becoming standard equipment among the more experienced traders.
¹ The present author notes that the Year Without a Summer — crop failures, famine prices, food riots — has made the disruption of supply chains by fairy intervention a political as well as a commercial problem. Merchants who can navigate both will be in demand.
title: Merchant or Tradesman
description: You trade goods in 1817 Europe — a famine year in a continent where fairy gold is entering markets and turning to leaves at dawn. Your healthy commercial suspicion of deals that seem too good is your best protection against Fae interference in the supply chain. Iron weights, rowan-wood scales, and the hard-won knowledge of what genuine quality looks like are your professional tools in a market that increasingly requires them.

keys: Journalist or Pamphleteer , the press correspondent ,
value:
Journalist or Pamphleteer. [See also: The Norrellite School, The Friends of English Magic, The History and Practice of English Magic, Books of Magic.]
You write for newspapers, magazines, or the broadsheet pamphleteering tradition — a vigorous, partisan, often scurrilous press that is the primary information system of 1817 Europe. Magic's return is the story of the century, and you are covering it, with the challenges particular to covering phenomena that can enchant your pen, deceive your sources, and produce events that reputable editors will not believe until they occur a third time. Norrell's periodical, The Friends of English Magic, represents one pole of magical journalism — controlled, institutional, and carefully edited to protect Mr. Norrell's monopoly. Strange's published work represents another. Your role is either to navigate between these positions or to choose one.
¹ The present author notes that the broadsheet trade in magical sensation has produced a great deal of material that is false, a moderate amount that is exaggerated, and a small but significant proportion that is accurate and has been dismissed on the grounds of appearing alongside the false and exaggerated material.
title: Journalist or Pamphleteer
description: You write about magic's return in an era of partisan, vigorous print journalism. The story is the most significant of the century. Your challenges include sources who are enchanted, editors who won't believe events until they recur, and the problem of distinguishing genuine supernatural occurrences from the considerable volume of fraud and fantasy that magic's return has also produced. Norrell controls one prominent magical publication. Strange has published a rival work. The press is, like the rest of English magic, fractured.

keys: Farmer or Rural Laborer , the husbandman ,
value:
Farmer or Rural Laborer. [See also: Genius Loci, Parliament of Domains, Baltic Sacred Fire Magic, Cunning Man or Wise Woman.]
You work the land — and the land has woken up. The field spirits are real. The harvest charms your grandmother taught you work, demonstrably and repeatedly. The standing stones at the edge of the north field are humming at a frequency that the cattle find unsettling. You have always lived closer to the old ways than any city-dweller, and magic's return is less shocking to you than to the educated classes — your grandmother always left milk out for the good folk, and the present situation is a revelation principally to the people who thought that was quaint. Your practical knowledge of land, season, and the spirits inhabiting specific fields is the most grounded magical education available. It is also the education that Mr. Norrell's school has no category for.
title: Farmer or Rural Laborer
description: You work the land, and the land is awake. The harvest charms your grandmother taught you are working. The standing stones at the field's edge are humming. Your practical knowledge of local spirits and seasonal observances — which educated people called superstition — turns out to be the most grounded magical education available. You are less surprised by magic's return than almost anyone, because you live closest to the things that have returned.

keys: Street Performer or Conjurer , the itinerant conjurer ,
value:
Street Performer or Conjurer. [See also: Fortune Teller, The Strangite School, Books of Magic.]
You made your living doing sleight of hand, card tricks, theatrical fortune-telling, and the performance of 'magic' for coins in market squares. You were a skilled fraud. Then magic returned, and your tricks began working without your assistance. The cards predicted things you had not engineered. The coins reappeared in impossible places independently. The spirits you pretended to summon answered. You have no formal training, no theoretical framework for what is happening, and an accidentally powerful gift acquired in circumstances that the established magical orders find both threatening and embarrassing. You are exactly the kind of practitioner Norrell has spent decades trying to prevent, which is to say, the kind that exists without his permission.
¹ The present author notes that several of the most effective practical magicians of the current period have backgrounds in performance rather than scholarship. This pattern has not been satisfactorily explained by either school.
title: Street Performer or Conjurer
description: You performed fake magic in market squares for coins, and then magic returned and your tricks started working without your intervention. You have no training, no theory, and no institutional standing — which means you have no framework for what is happening, and also no Norrell monitoring your work. The established magical schools find your existence both threatening and difficult to categorise. You are the kind of practitioner the profession's gatekeepers most wanted to prevent.

keys: Criminal or Smuggler , the gentleman of the road ,
value:
Criminal or Smuggler. [See also: Fairy Debt, The Mélusine Court, Books of Magic, Books as Objects of Power.]
You operate outside the law — theft, smuggling, forgery, confidence games. Magic's return has created a black market in magical goods, services, and knowledge that your existing networks, risk tolerance, and moral flexibility position you perfectly for. Fairy-touched objects command considerable prices. Stolen grimoires are worth fortunes. Forged protective amulets endanger the people who buy them, which is the kind of problem that tends to resolve itself without your involvement. The danger: magical crime attracts magical consequences, and the Fae courts have their own notions of justice that operate independently of mortal law. Being pursued by a magistrate is inconvenient. Being pursued by the Mélusine Court's legal process is a different category of problem entirely.
¹ The Mélusine Court does not regard the lack of a signed contract as an obstacle to litigation. It regards it as the claim.
title: Criminal or Smuggler
description: You operate outside the law, and magic's return has opened a black market of extraordinary opportunity — stolen grimoires, fairy-touched objects, protection services that actually work. Your networks, your comfort with risk, and your flexibility about the rules are genuine advantages. The hazard: magical crime draws magical consequences, and the Fae courts enforce their own versions of property law without reference to any mortal jurisdiction.

keys: Midwife , the monthly nurse ,
value:
Midwife. [See also: Caul-Born, Seventh Son or Seventh Daughter, Táltos-Born, Hound of God, Fairy Debt.]
You deliver babies — and in doing so, you are the first to know whether a child is born with a caul, with teeth, with extra fingers, or with any of the dozen birth-marks that indicate a supernatural destiny. You occupy the liminal space of birth: the threshold between existence and nonexistence, the moment when whatever a soul is enters the world. This liminal position has always carried a whiff of the uncanny in every tradition. Now that magic has returned, you are the gatekeeper. You identify the marked children. You perform the protective rituals. You decide what to tell the parents and what to keep to yourself. You have always made this decision; you simply now understand its full consequences.
¹ The midwife's catalogue of the births she has attended, kept carefully over a career, constitutes one of the most valuable records of supernatural marking currently in existence. Several persons would pay considerably for such a record. The midwife is advised to consider carefully to whom, if anyone, she shows it.
title: Midwife
description: You deliver babies and are therefore the first to know which children bear the marks that indicate supernatural destiny — caul-born, táltos-marked, seventh children. You stand at the most liminal threshold available: the moment of arrival. With magic's return you have become a gatekeeper with genuine power over who knows what about which children. The knowledge you accumulate across a career is extraordinary and coveted. Deciding who gets it is now a significant part of your work.

keys: Folklorist or Antiquarian , the curious antiquarian ,
value:
Folklorist or Antiquarian. [See also: Books of Magic, Scholar or Archivist, The Reawakening of Magic, Jacob Grimm, Walter Scott, Elias Lönnrot.]
You collect stories, songs, customs, and beliefs — the living oral tradition of your region. The Brothers Grimm are your most famous contemporaries, but every nation has its collectors: Lönnrot compiling the Kalevala, Scott recording Border ballads, antiquarians documenting local customs before industrialisation erases them. You treated this work as scholarship, preservation, perhaps romantic nationalism. Then magic returned, and your field notes became operational intelligence. The fairy tale you transcribed from a grandmother in a Hessian village is a genuine account of a fairy encounter. The charm you recorded as quaint superstition is a working spell. The song you notated for its unusual metre is a functional incantation. You are not a magician, but you hold a library of magical knowledge you collected without realising what it was — and several parties who do realise are now very interested in your notebooks.
¹ The Grimm brothers have been summoned to Berlin by the Prussian War Ministry. The present compiler draws no conclusions from this but observes that folklorists are rarely summoned by War Ministries for scholarly reasons.
title: Folklorist or Antiquarian
description: You collect folklore — stories, songs, charms, customs — treating them as cultural preservation or romantic scholarship. Then magic returned, and your field notes turned out to be genuine accounts of fairy encounters and working spells. You are not a magician, but your notebooks contain knowledge that magicians desperately need. The Brothers Grimm, Walter Scott, and the young Elias Lönnrot are your most famous contemporaries. Several interested parties would very much like to see what you have written down.

keys: Writer or Poet , the literary gentleman ,
value:
Writer or Poet. [See also: The Madness Axis, Books of Magic, The Strangite School, The Romantic Movement.]
This is the age of Romanticism — of Byron, Shelley, Keats, and their continental counterparts. Mary Shelley is completing Frankenstein in 1817; Jane Austen has just died. You write poetry, fiction, or essays, and you have discovered that in a world where magic is real, words have literal power that their authors did not intend. A poem written with genuine feeling and precise language can produce effects that surprise the person who wrote it. A novel that captures something true about the supernatural world may function as a grimoire. The Romantic movement's obsession with the sublime and the power of imagination has accidentally produced a generation of literary near-magicians, most of whom are currently very alarmed by what their pens are doing.
¹ Strange has published a work of practical magic that reads, in places, like a very unusual natural history. Several of its descriptions have produced phenomena in readers who attempted to replicate them. Strange has been informed of this. His response has not been reported.
title: Writer or Poet
description: You write in the Romantic era — when literature is obsessed with the sublime, the uncanny, and the power of imagination — and you have discovered that imagination, in 1817, has more power than the word implied. A poem written with genuine precision can produce effects you did not intend. A novel that captures something true about the supernatural world may work as a grimoire. The Romantic movement accidentally prepared a generation of writers for this. Most of them are alarmed.

keys: The United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland , Great Britain ,
value:
The United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland. [See also: English Practical Magic, Captain James Fairfax, The Raven King's Roads Reopen, The Norrellite/Strangite Split, The Raven King's Court.]
The crossing is at Dover, where Captain James Fairfax commands a customs inspection service whose official mandate concerns goods and whose actual mandate concerns everything else that crosses the Channel. He carries iron-tipped ammunition, a rowan-wood baton, and a well-annotated copy of Strange's History and Practice of English Magic. He will want to know your tradition, your court allegiances if any, and whether you carry anything that responds to iron. He is methodical rather than hostile. The answer to his last question is always relevant.
Beyond Dover: the world's preeminent naval power in domestic crisis. Habeas corpus suspended. Famine, the Corn Laws, food riots. The Raven King's fairy roads reopening across the north, people accidentally wandering into them. English magical society split between Norrell's institutional school and Strange's wild practice, with a third tradition — women's magic, invisible to both factions — running quietly beneath both. The Thames has spoken aloud once. The genius loci are paying attention.
¹ The Gentleman's face has been seen in London mirrors. Childermass is increasingly acting on his own judgment. York — The Learned Society in Crisis is in crisis. These three facts are connected in ways no one has yet articulated publicly.
title: The United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland
description: Britain in 1817 is the birthplace of magic's return — where two rival magicians accidentally reawakened a tradition dormant for four centuries. It's also in acute crisis: famine, political repression, and fairy roads opening across northern England. Whoever you are, arriving here puts you at the centre of the most consequential magical development in centuries, and inside a factional war between two schools that have very different ideas about what to do about it.

keys: The Kingdom of France , France ,
value:
The Kingdom of France. [See also: French Grimoire Magic, Sergeant Émile Garnier, The Catacomb Breach, The Mélusine Court, The Ankou's Census.]
The crossing is at Calais, or at Bayonne on the Basque frontier, depending on direction. Sergeant Émile Garnier commands whichever post you arrive at — a man of forty-eight with considerable experience of human nature and a set of standing orders regarding 'les phénomènes extraordinaires' that he has read three times and understood not at all. He is pragmatic: he will wave you through if you appear manageable and detain you if you appear interesting. He is more afraid of the paperwork than of the phenomena.
Beyond the border: a Bourbon restoration draped over republican memory over revolutionary trauma. Paris's grimoire tradition means practitioners can buy operational texts from certain bookstalls on the Seine. The catacombs beneath the city have breached into the Mélusine Court's Grimoire Vault and Parisian urchins are selling fairy documents of uncertain safety. In Brittany, the Ankou has begun counting the living rather than collecting the dead. The Stones of Carnac are humming. The korrigans will not explain why.
¹ The Mélusine Court has no view on French politics. It has filed a request for clarification on which government currently holds the relevant contracts. No reply has been received. It considers silence a binding admission of default.
title: The Kingdom of France
description: France in 1817 is politically fractured between royalists, Bonapartists, and republicans — and now has a fourth axis of dispute. The grimoire tradition makes French practitioners unusually well-supplied with magical texts that are now operational. The Mélusine Court runs the most legalistic Fae bureaucracy in Europe, and the catacombs have just opened a door into its library that nobody authorised.

keys: The Austrian Empire , the Habsburg dominions ,
value:
The Austrian Empire. [See also: Balkan Dragon-Scholar Magic, Herr Franz Gruber, The Táltos War, The Scholomance Court, Doktor Sigismund Hartmann.]
The crossing is at the Semmering Pass or the Brenner, depending on approach. Herr Franz Gruber is the customs inspector — a man of forty-one with an obsequious manner, a suspicious mind, and a checklist of 'indicators of extraordinary activity' whose terminology he cannot source in any available dictionary. He confiscates anything that looks magical and files a report forwarded to Vienna and, as far as he can determine, read by no one. He has seventeen confiscated items in a locked box at the customs house that occasionally hums. He has not yet filed a report on the humming.
Beyond the border: the most multi-ethnic empire in Europe, held together by dynasty, bureaucracy, and the Congress of Vienna. Magic's return has intensified every centrifugal force it contains. Two Hungarian táltos are fighting as flaming bulls over the plain and the storms are devastating farmland across three counties. The Scholomance has reopened in the Transylvanian Alps. Croatian krsnik fight vampires without consulting the Imperial Chancellery. Doktor Hartmann's private journal documents everything the Commission's official reports suppress.
¹ Metternich's apparatus has generated approximately four hundred documents classifying supernatural phenomena since 1815. The phenomena have not been informed of their classifications and have not modified their conduct accordingly.
title: The Austrian Empire
description: The Habsburg Empire governs Germans, Hungarians, Czechs, Romanians, Italians, Poles, and others — each with their own magical tradition and their own growing restlessness. Magic's return has intensified every tension the empire contains. The bureaucratic response is thorough, well-documented, and entirely ineffective. The Scholomance is accepting applications in the Transylvanian Alps.

keys: The Russian Empire , Russia ,
value:
The Russian Empire. [See also: Slavic Whisper Magic, Marfa Ivanovna, The Leshy Territorial Expansion, The Parliament of Domains, Father Alexei Volkonsky.]
The crossing is managed in theory by the official customs post. In practice, Marfa Ivanovna — a znakharka of approximately seventy in the nearest village — determines who is safe to let through. The actual officials defer to her judgment without admitting it publicly. She will feed you, ask about your grandmother's magical practices, and decide within a few minutes of your arrival whether you carry fairy taint, an unreckoned pact-debt, or something worse. Warmth and honesty about your own tradition appear to help.
Beyond the border: an empire of extraordinary size where magic's return manifests differently in St. Petersburg drawing rooms and Siberian forests. The leshy of the Białowieża Forest has extended his territorial claim; farmers wake to find trees where there were fields. The domovoi in every hearth, the vodyanoy in every river, are awake and demanding the old courtesies. The zagovory whispered over sick children are the same from Novgorod to Vladivostok. Father Volkonsky in St. Petersburg sees things correctly. His superiors are not asking him what he sees.
¹ The Tsar's mystical inclinations are being overtaken by events considerably stranger than his theology prepared him for. Prince Golitsyn has added a new filing category to his administrative system. He has not told anyone what it is called.
title: The Russian Empire
description: Russia is vast, autocratic, and haunted — an empire where folk magic is inseparable from daily life, every hearth has a domovoi, every forest has a leshy, and they are all awake. The Tsar is a mystic grappling with forces his theology did not prepare him for. The forests are remembering how large they used to be before human settlement reduced them, and the reduction is being reversed.

keys: The Kingdom of Prussia , Prussia ,
value:
The Kingdom of Prussia. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, Leutnant Karl von Steinberg, The Grimm Emergency, The Æsir Court, Professor Heinrich Brandt.]
The crossing is monitored by the Abteilung für Außergewöhnliche Aufklärung — the Department for Extraordinary Reconnaissance — represented by Leutnant Karl von Steinberg, who is twenty-four, precise, and carrying a leather case of Professor Brandt's detection instruments, none of which work reliably. He will measure you. He will categorise you according to a system with twenty-four categories and a fortieth-percentile 'further study required.' He is not hostile; he is relentlessly procedural, which practitioners occasionally find more difficult to manage than hostility.
Beyond the border: the most militarily efficient state in Europe, applying its characteristic discipline to the magical question. A fairy tale collected in Kassel by the Brothers Grimm has activated — a Napoleonic veteran in the village is now living its plot step by step, unable to deviate. The Grimms have been summoned to Berlin. A Viking-era runestone in the Antiquarian Society's collection has begun glowing and producing compulsive truthfulness within a ten-foot radius. Professor Brandt is professionally ecstatic about this.
¹ Prussia treats magic as it treats everything — as a tool of state power — without fully reckoning with the fact that the sources of that power have their own agendas and no particular interest in Prussian organizational charts.
title: The Kingdom of Prussia
description: The most militarily disciplined state in Europe is applying Prussian thoroughness to the magical question — systematic study, military application, and a classification system that covers everything except the forty percent of observed phenomena it files as 'further study required.' The Brothers Grimm's fairy tale collections have been reclassified as intelligence reports. One of them has activated.

keys: The Kingdom of Spain , Spain ,
value:
The Kingdom of Spain. [See also: Iberian Blood-and-Stone Magic, Padre Diego Montero, The Inquisition's Dilemma, The Mari Court, Pétalo Heredia.]
The crossing is in the Pyrenees, where Padre Diego Montero — lay officer of the Holy Office, fifty years old, possessing genuine authority to detain — monitors the frontier for practitioners of brujería. He is devout, thoroughgoing, and privately troubled: a man who believes his faith, follows his orders, and is increasingly uncertain the two point in the same direction. The bruja in Logroño performed genuine magic before his tribunal and the fire she called still burns in the hearth. He has sent his report to Madrid. Ferdinand VII has not responded. Montero will ask you your religion first and your purpose second.
Beyond the border: an empire in decline, its Inquisition recently restored and genuinely dangerous. The Basque Country is something else entirely — the Mari Court's tradition predates Indo-European languages, and Mari has spoken directly from every cave mouth in the Pyrenees. The basajaun has been sighted for the first time in centuries. Pétalo Heredia's horse-intelligence network covers Spain more comprehensively than any courier system the government operates.
¹ Iberian practitioners have centuries of experience working under institutions with judicial powers. This is a transferable skill. The present author recommends acquiring it before crossing.
title: The Kingdom of Spain
description: Spain has a functioning Inquisition and the oldest Fae court in Western Europe — both awake, both active, and neither answerable to the other. The Mari Court of the Basque Country predates Indo-European languages. The Inquisition's records date to 1478. They are currently arresting people for magic that is demonstrably real, without a theological framework for what to do about it.

keys: The Ottoman Empire , the Sublime Porte ,
value:
The Ottoman Empire. [See also: Desert Magic, Yusuf Efendi, The Jinn Question, The Djinn Courts, Elif Hatun.]
The crossing is the Danube or the Adriatic coast, managed by Yusuf Efendi — a dragoman whose linguistic repertoire covers Turkish, Greek, French, Italian, and Arabic, and whose function is to facilitate passage while assessing 'unusual capabilities.' His instructions specify the need to assess and are admirably vague about what to do with the results. He is cosmopolitan, educated, and simultaneously welcoming and evaluating in proportions calibrated to be nearly indistinguishable from pure hospitality.
Beyond the border: a civilizational crossroads spanning southeastern Europe, Anatolia, and the Middle East. Ottoman scholars are currently arguing whether European Fae courts are simply jinn — already accounted for in Islamic theology. The Djinn are offended by the classification. The Olympians are offended by the denial of kinship. The Hagia Sophia has begun producing a harmonic tone that makes worshippers of all faiths simultaneously feel personally observed. Elif Hatun in Constantinople's Grand Bazaar can diagnose the Evil Eye across four cultural traditions simultaneously and has been doing so since before magic returned.
¹ The Ottoman approach — engagement through Sufi mysticism rather than suppression — represents a fundamentally different response to magic's return than any Western European model. It has produced better results. Western European governments have not yet noticed this.
title: The Ottoman Empire
description: The Ottoman Empire spans the intersection of European magic, Islamic mysticism, and the ancient Djinn Courts. It governs territories where the Scholomance, the Olympian Court, and Slavic spirits all operate simultaneously. The Hagia Sophia is audibly remembering all the sacred purposes it has served at once. This is a more sophisticated approach to magic's return than most European states have managed.

keys: The Kingdom of Sweden-Norway , the Northern Kingdoms ,
value:
The Kingdom of Sweden-Norway. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, Fru Astrid Lindqvist, The Draugr Rising, The Æsir Court, Eiríkr the Walker.]
The crossing is at Svinesund on the Norwegian-Swedish frontier, where Fru Astrid Lindqvist is the wise woman the village defers to — a klok gumma of sixty who has practiced from her svarteboken since her mother taught her at twelve. She reads runes with alarming accuracy. She charges a fair price in silver or labour and considers this an important point of principle: knowledge has cost her forty-eight years of practice and ought not be given away. She will tell you what she sees bluntly, because she finds tact more trouble than bluntness causes. If you are carrying something that shouldn't enter the country, she will know.
Beyond the border: draugar are emerging from grave-mounds across Norway and Sweden — standing at barrow entrances, looking out at a world they do not recognise, waiting for orders from a commander who has not arrived. The Æsir Court held an unscheduled assembly on the Brocken; something frightened them. A new rune has appeared on Yggdrasil that the One-Eyed Lord did not carve. Iceland — technically a Danish dependency — is the unexpected centre of the Northern European magical revival.
¹ Eiríkr the Walker, a conscious draugr in the One-Eyed Lord's service, has been observed near Kronborg Castle twice this month. He has not spoken to anyone. He walks with the patience of a man who has already seen whatever is about to happen.
title: The Kingdom of Sweden-Norway
description: Scandinavia is the heartland of Norse magical tradition — the region that kept the most complete records of the Æsir Court's expectations and never entirely stopped believing in the beings those records describe. The draugar are rising from their grave-mounds. Something new has appeared on Yggdrasil. The Wild Hunt rides winter storms. Iceland is the unexpected centre of the revival.

keys: The Papal States , Rome ,
value:
The Papal States. [See also: Mediterranean Eye-Ward Magic, Brother Matteo, The Benandanti Return, Religion versus Magic, Padre Tommaso Sforza.]
The crossing at the Papal States' frontier is managed by a small Franciscan hospice where Brother Matteo — twenty-eight, earnest, gentle — maintains genuine hospitality as a religious obligation. He asks every traveler whether they have experienced anything unusual on the road. He has been asking for eight months. He has filled two notebooks with the answers. He has shown them to no one and does not know who to show them to without being reassigned to a house with no border traffic.
Beyond the border: the seat of the Catholic Church, facing the most acute theological crisis in Catholic history since the Reformation. In Friuli, hundreds of caul-born Italians are leaving their bodies on Ember Days to fight organised opposition in spirit form — something has organised the malandanti, the opposing spirits, for the first time. In the Vatican's archives, Padre Sforza has discovered that certain cardinals across three centuries knew magic was real and suppressed the evidence. A grimoire sealed in 1493 has opened itself and is reading itself aloud in the King's Letters.
¹ Official doctrine holds that all supernatural phenomena are either divine miracles or demonic. The lived reality of February 1817 fits neither category. The Church is debating how to respond. The phenomena are not waiting for the debate to conclude.
title: The Papal States
description: The seat of the Catholic Church is in acute theological crisis. The Inquisition's framework requires all magic to be either divine miracle or demonic fraud. In 1817, a caul-born spirit militia is fighting organised supernatural opposition across Friuli, and a medieval grimoire in the Vatican archives has opened itself.
