The official classification system has no category for either.

keys: The Kingdoms of Denmark and Iceland , Denmark ,
value:
The Kingdoms of Denmark and Iceland. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, Bjarni Jónsson, The Galdrabók Plague, The Æsir Court, Sigríður Kolsdóttir.]
Denmark proper: a small, cosmopolitan kingdom adjusting to the loss of Norway. Entry through Copenhagen, where Bjarni Jónsson — an Icelandic scholar and manuscript trader of thirty-five — can arrange passage to Iceland for those with legitimate scholarly interest and sufficient funds. He deals in galdrabækur and galdrastafir templates. Business is excellent. He is knowledgeable, opportunistic, and careful about not selling to anyone he believes will use the material badly.
Iceland is a different matter entirely: the unexpected centre of the Northern European magical revival. Galdrastafir are appearing spontaneously across the island — carved overnight into doorposts, scratched into windowsills, etched into pond-ice — as if Iceland itself is warding its population against something anticipated. The handwriting is consistent across all instances. Bjarni has compared them and not yet shared his conclusion about whose hand it resembles. Kronborg Castle in Elsinore sits on a maritime gate, and what sleeps beneath it turned over in its sleep last night.
¹ Sigríður Kolsdóttir guards the threshold between the mortal world and the Æsir's halls — a dead völva in the One-Eyed Lord's service, who tests visitors with riddles and is said to find the experience of striking contemptuous visitors with lightning regrettable rather than pleasurable.
title: The Kingdoms of Denmark and Iceland
description: Denmark is a small kingdom; Iceland is its remote Atlantic dependency and the unexpected capital of the Norse magical revival. Iceland's medieval manuscripts — the Eddas, the sagas, the grimoires called galdrabækur — are the most complete surviving records of the Æsir Court's expectations. The Icelandic population never entirely stopped believing in the hidden people. They have been vindicated.

keys: The Grand Duchy of Lithuania , Lithuania ,
value:
The Grand Duchy of Lithuania. [See also: Baltic Sacred Fire Magic, Vytautas the Flame-Carrier, The Fire Spreads, The Romuva Court, Dalia Kazlauskienė.]
Lithuania is technically a part of the Russian Empire. The border is a Russian military post. But the more consequential arrival point is the clearing where Dalia Kazlauskienė — a grandmother of approximately seventy, formerly a schoolteacher's wife — has been tending the rekindled sacred fire of Romuva since she found it burning in a clearing her grandmother had described in stories four generations old. She sits with it. She feeds it oak. She greets visitors with the same hospitality: sit, warm yourself, show proper respect.
Beyond the border: the Romuva Court had barely closed its eyes before magic's return jolted it awake. The sacred fire at Romuva has spawned daughter-fires at six additional grove sites. Vytautas the Flame-Carrier moves between them with his sacred lantern, carrying messages from Perkūnas. Russian authorities have dispatched a military detachment. Juozas Vaitkevičius is trying to reach the groves with his notebooks before the soldiers do. Living grandmothers remember the songs. The grove-sites are intact. The thunder-being's lightning has been striking church steeples with improving accuracy.
¹ The tradition has had four centuries of suppression. It is making up for the interval with what the present author can only describe as geological thoroughness.
title: The Grand Duchy of Lithuania
description: Lithuania was the last pagan nation in Europe, not Christianized until 1387, and the old tradition was so recently buried that living grandmothers remember it whole. The Romuva Court barely had time to sleep before magic returned and woke it. Sacred fires are rekindling across the countryside. The thunder-being wants his groves and prayers back. Russian authorities are alarmed. The tradition does not care.

keys: London , the Metropolis ,
value:
London. [See also: English Practical Magic, Mrs. Amelia Carstairs, London — The Thames Speaks, The Norrellite/Strangite Split, Stephen Black.]
Population approximately 1.1 million — the largest city in Europe and the capital of the world's most powerful empire. London is ground zero for the English magical revival, and it is simultaneously in acute domestic crisis: habeas corpus suspended, famine, food riots, the Corn Laws keeping grain prices ruinous. The Thames spoke aloud last month — a single deep syllable that shattered windows in Westminster and stopped every clock in Southwark. Strange was at the riverbank the following morning, writing in the King's Letters. The Gentleman's face has been seen in London mirrors. The city has an invisible magical community operating below the Learned Society's notice: fortune-tellers whose readings are now genuine, street magicians whose tricks have started working, practitioners from traditions the English establishment cannot categorise. A Strangite underground press in Cheapside produces unauthorised copies of suppressed texts. Drawlight is trying to locate it. Mrs. Carstairs's salon in Mayfair is the network's hub.
¹ Harley Street harbours Stephen Black's enchantment. Hanover Square harbours Norrell, Lascelles, and Drawlight. Between these two houses, the English magical crisis is being enacted in miniature.
title: London
description: The largest city in Europe and the centre of the English magical revival — where two rival magicians have split the magical establishment into warring factions, where the Thames recently spoke aloud, and where an invisible underground of practitioners operates below the official debate. The world's most powerful empire is trying to understand what magic means for its power, and magic is not making it easy.

keys: Paris , the French capital ,
value:
Paris. [See also: French Grimoire Magic, Monsieur Delacroix, Paris — The Catacomb Breach, The Mélusine Court, Mademoiselle Lenormand.]
Population approximately 700,000. A city of contradictions — Bourbon restoration draped over revolutionary memory. The return of magic has added another layer: the catacombs beneath the city have breached into the Mélusine Court's Grimoire Vault and the entry point sits beneath Monsieur Delacroix's bookshop on the Quai des Grands-Augustins. He has barricaded it and is demanding hazard pay from parties he declines to specify. Parisian urchins are selling 'genuine fairy documents' at least two of which are genuine and at least one of which creates obligations for whoever touches it. Mademoiselle Lenormand's salon on the rue de Tournon is the city's most consequential intelligence network: she advised Napoleon's court, she has been imprisoned twice for predicting inconvenient events, and her card readings are now producing information she cannot explain and did not seek. The Mélusine Court's legalistic Fae treat Paris as their capital. The Chief Notary has filed a request for clarification on which government currently holds the relevant contracts.
¹ Germaine de Staël, the most formidable intellect in Paris, will suffer a stroke on 21 February 1817. She is, in early February, at the absolute height of her powers. Time to speak with her is running out.
title: Paris
description: The revolutionary capital of Europe, now under Bourbon restoration, with its catacombs breached into a fairy court's underground library. The grimoire tradition makes Parisian practitioners unusually literate in magic that is now operational. The city's most famous fortune-teller advised Napoleon and has been imprisoned twice for accuracy. The Mélusine Court considers Paris its mortal capital.

keys: Vienna , the imperial seat ,
value:
Vienna. [See also: Táltos, Doktor Sigismund Hartmann, Vienna — The Musical Phenomenon, The Scholomance Court, Ludwig van Beethoven.]
Population approximately 250,000 — seat of the Habsburg Empire and the Congress of Vienna's host city. Metternich's Austria is dedicated to stability and the suppression of anything that threatens it. The Viennese response has been characteristically bureaucratic: a Commission for Extraordinary Phenomena staffed by minor aristocrats, producing reports no one reads. Doktor Hartmann maintains a separate private journal documenting everything the official reports suppress. He is looking for someone he can trust with the information. A performance of Don Giovanni at the Burgtheater produced a supernatural event — the stone guest actually appeared, in living marble — and what it summoned has not left the theatre. Beethoven, informed of this, asked for the full account, listened without comment, and returned to his notebooks. Schubert's Schubertiad evenings are producing atmospheric effects that cannot be attributed to acoustics. He writes what he hears. He has not elaborated on what he hears it from.
¹ The táltos war on the Hungarian plain is generating storms visible across three counties. Habsburg authorities cannot locate the physical bodies of the combatants. Doktor Hartmann has been dispatched. He is documenting what he cannot explain with the honesty that makes his private journal so much more useful than his official reports.
title: Vienna
description: The political capital of post-Napoleonic Europe, where Metternich's system of conservative order meets magic's return with systematic bureaucratic denial. The Commission for Extraordinary Phenomena files reports no one reads. Meanwhile, a stone guest has appeared in a theatre and failed to leave, a composer is writing music that does things music should not do, and the empire's Hungarian subjects are fighting supernatural battles that produce visible storms.

keys: St. Petersburg , Petersburg ,
value:
St. Petersburg. [See also: Slavic Whisper Magic, Father Alexei Volkonsky, St. Petersburg — The Neva Freezes Wrong, The Parliament of Domains, Tsar Alexander I.]
Population approximately 360,000 — the artificial city built on a swamp by Peter the Great's imperial will, and always haunted. The Neva River has frozen in a pattern visible from the Winter Palace's upper windows that Father Volkonsky identifies as a word in a Slavic script he does not recognise. The Tsar's advisors are arguing about whether to read it, destroy it, or pretend it is not there. Father Volkonsky, whose forty years of hesychast discipline now produce reliable supernatural perception, recommends reading it: a thing that writes itself in the capital's principal river is unlikely to be satisfied with being ignored. His recommendation has not been adopted. The pattern has not melted. The vodyanoy of the Neva are active. The domovoi in the Winter Palace are restless. Prince Golitsyn, Minister of Religious Affairs, has added a new filing category to his administrative system and has not told anyone what it is called.
¹ The Tsar's mystical instincts — guided by the dying Jung-Stilling and the millenarian Baroness von Krüdener — were always pointing at something real. He was wrong about what it was. He is beginning to understand this, and the understanding is not comfortable.
title: St. Petersburg
description: The artificial city built on a swamp, always described as haunted, is now demonstrably so. The Neva has written a word in its ice. The Tsar's government is trying to decide whether to read it, suppress it, or pretend it isn't there. The Parliament of Domains' spirits are active in every waterway and hearth of the city. The Orthodox Church is institutionally conservative and practically overwhelmed.

keys: Constantinople , Stamboul ,
value:
Constantinople. [See also: Desert Magic, Elif Hatun, Constantinople — The Hagia Sophia Resonance, The Djinn Courts, Yusuf Efendi.]
Population approximately 500,000 — the crossroads of Europe and Asia. The Hagia Sophia is producing a continuous harmonic tone in a register that makes listeners feel simultaneously devout and terrified. Muslim, Christian, and Jewish worshippers all report the same experience: vast, ancient attention, personally directed. Elif Hatun, whose apothecary in the Grand Bazaar has been the city's discreet magical consultation centre for decades, says the building is 'remembering what it was' — all its sacred purposes activating at once. She can diagnose the Evil Eye across four cultural traditions simultaneously and has been doing so since before magic returned. Khalid ibn Rashid visits regularly from Damascus: the most experienced Djinn negotiator in the eastern Mediterranean, exhausted by the volume of families arriving with debts they did not know they carried. Zahra bint Yusuf has sent word from Cairo that she is working as quickly as she can on the seal reconstruction.
¹ Constantinople sits where the Olympian Court's territory meets the Djinn's — two ancient powers with a history of conflict, now both fully awake, and a city of half a million mortals between them. Yusuf Efendi is attempting to mediate. He finds the task clarifying.
title: Constantinople
description: The seat of the Ottoman Empire sits at the intersection of European Fae courts and the Djinn's ancient territory, at a city layered with sacred power accumulated across two thousand years. The Hagia Sophia is audibly remembering all its previous incarnations simultaneously. The Grand Bazaar has always been a supernatural crossroads. In 1817 this description has become literal.

keys: Rome , the Eternal City ,
value:
Rome. [See also: Mediterranean Eye-Ward Magic, Padre Tommaso Sforza, Rome — The Archive Speaks, Religion versus Magic, Pope Pius VII.]
Population approximately 150,000 — the seat of the Catholic Church, a city where two thousand years of sacred and profane magic coexist in every stone. A grimoire in the Vatican's suppressed collection has opened itself. Sealed with wax, iron, and a papal blessing in 1493 — the seal is intact, but the book is open, its pages turning as if read by an invisible scholar, displaying text in the King's Letters identical to the script on Vinculus's body. Padre Sforza has been watching it for three days and has not submitted his report. He is composing it carefully, aware that the version he submits will determine what happens next and that he has only one opportunity to get the framing right. Pope Pius VII knows. He has been praying about it. The prayers are producing results — not the results he anticipated. The catacombs beneath Rome are considerably older and more extensive than Paris's, and connect to spaces that are not entirely physical.
¹ The Italian folk traditions — strega, fattucchiera, benandanti — have always operated in the Church's shadow. Magic's return has made the shadow visible. The Church is debating whether the light revealing it is divine or demonic. This is taking longer than the circumstances comfortably permit.
title: Rome
description: The seat of the Catholic Church is facing an unprecedented theological crisis. A Raven King's grimoire in the Vatican archives has opened itself and is reading aloud. The Pope is praying about it. The folk traditions the Church has spent centuries classifying as demonic are demonstrably effective, and two thousand years of accumulated sacred power is active in every stone of a city where nothing has ever entirely stayed buried.

keys: Berlin , the Prussian capital ,
value:
Berlin. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, Professor Heinrich Brandt, Berlin — The Rune-Stone Activates, The Æsir Court, Jacob Grimm.]
Population approximately 200,000 — capital of the most militarily efficient state in Europe. The University of Berlin has established a Chair of Extraordinary Natural Philosophy, occupied by Professor Brandt, who is thirty-two, brilliant, and deeply frustrated by magic's refusal to behave according to predictable principles. A Viking-era runestone in the Antiquarian Society's collection has begun glowing. The runes are functional magical text, producing compulsive truthfulness within a ten-foot radius, inability to carry iron, and a persistent sensation of being watched. Brandt is professionally ecstatic. Jacob Grimm, summoned for the Grimm Emergency, passed through the room, read the inscription, and spent seventeen minutes writing in a notebook he has declined to show anyone. E.T.A. Hoffmann writes his supernatural fiction in the same city by day he serves as a court official — his accuracy rate is now alarming his editors. Leutnant von Steinberg files classification reports. The Prussian army is integrating magical capabilities into its order of battle.
¹ The One-Eyed Lord's position on Prussian organizational charts: he has not been informed of them and would find the concept amusing if he were.
title: Berlin
description: Prussia's capital is approaching magic with characteristic Prussian efficiency — systematic study, military application, and a classification system that covers most of what it encounters. A glowing runestone in the Antiquarian Society is producing compulsive truthfulness in a ten-foot radius, which is making the nearby meetings very interesting. Germany's greatest Gothic horror writer serves as a court official here by day.

keys: Edinburgh , Auld Reekie ,
value:
Edinburgh. [See also: Celtic Otherworld Magic, Dr. Margaret Buchanan, Edinburgh — Arthur's Seat Opens, The Sídhe Court, Walter Scott.]
Population approximately 120,000 — the Athens of the North, capital of the Scottish Enlightenment, and a city with one foot in rational philosophy and the other in Highland fairy tradition. Arthur's Seat — the extinct volcano at the city's heart — has fissured, revealing a passage into the hill's interior. Warm air carries the sound of music. Dr. Margaret Buchanan has examined the opening with her Sight and reports a 'substantial fairy presence' within. The city council is debating whether to post guards, seal the entrance, or charge admission. Walter Scott has visited twice and taken notes. James Hogg, who grew up genuinely believing his mother's fairy stories, says the failure to send a formal delegation is exactly the kind of thing the Fair Family finds both discourteous and useful. Edinburgh's medical schools, the finest in Europe, are attempting to apply scientific method to supernatural phenomena. Dr. Buchanan is a physician with second sight who has been practicing both for twenty years and desperately needs colleagues who understand both halves of her working world.
¹ Edinburgh's unique position between English institutional magic and Highland Celtic tradition makes it the natural meeting-ground for practitioners of both. Both traditions are currently certain they are correct. Dr. Buchanan is treating patients from both. She has views.
title: Edinburgh
description: Edinburgh sits between the Enlightenment and the fairy tradition — the most rational city in Britain, with a fairy court awakening in its volcanic heart. Arthur's Seat has opened. The finest medical schools in Europe are applying scientific method to supernatural phenomena. A physician with second sight has been waiting years for colleagues who understand what she sees. The Highland Clearances are scattering knowledge that Edinburgh is only beginning to understand it needs.

keys: Hurtfew Abbey Yorkshire , Norrell's abbey ,
value:
Hurtfew Abbey, Yorkshire. [See also: Gilbert Norrell, Books as Objects of Power, Hurtfew Abbey — The Missing Chapter, The Norrellite/Strangite Split, John Childermass.]
The private residence and library of Gilbert Norrell — the most complete collection of English magical texts in existence. Norrell has spent decades purchasing or suppressing every genuine spellbook he could find; Hurtfew is the accumulated result. A page has appeared in one of his most guarded volumes, written in the King's Letters and describing a spell he has never encountered. It was not there yesterday. He has verified this three times and will not verify it a fourth. The Abbey sits on a ley-line through the Raven King's old territory, and the concentrated knowledge within its walls generates a palpable aura of power. Gaining access is one of the most valuable achievements an English-tradition practitioner can pursue. Norrell knows every book he owns. He notices absences. Childermass's growing independence means the gatekeeping is not quite what it was — and Childermass knows where the sealed report on the King's madness is filed.
¹ Mrs. Lennox, Norrell's housekeeper, has managed the household through decades of magical practice. She identifies rooms where magic has been performed by smell — a faint scent of lightning — and has a considerably more complete picture of Hurtfew's operations than Norrell has elected to share with anyone.
title: Hurtfew Abbey, Yorkshire
description: The greatest private library of magical texts in England, and the seat of Mr. Norrell's power. Getting in is difficult. Getting access to anything useful within it is harder. Every book Norrell has locked away is a spell that does not exist in the world. A page written in the Raven King's script has appeared in a book that did not previously contain one, and Norrell has not yet decided what this means.

keys: Venice Italy , the Serenissima ,
value:
Venice, Italy. [See also: Jonathan Strange, Venice — Strange's Mirror Experiments, The Madness Axis, Flora Greysteel, Lord Byron.]
Jonathan Strange is here, grieving his wife's apparent death, performing experiments with Venetian mirrors that are producing effects far beyond his lodgings. Canal reflections move independently of what they reflect. Shop windows occasionally show Lost-hope's ballroom. The Gentleman's face appears in reflective surfaces across the city and is gone before certainty. Flora Greysteel — practical, perceptive, brave enough to stay near a brilliant man in the late stages of private collapse — is his primary link to sanity. Byron, attending Carnival, reports the masked balls feel 'haunted by a music no one can quite hear.' Venice is architecturally a mirror-maze: every canal a reflective surface, every window facing water. The King's Roads have more potential entry points here per square mile than anywhere else in Europe, which is not coincidental — grief pushed Strange toward the experiments, and the experiments led him here.
¹ Strange is on the verge of deliberately inducing madness to access fairy sight. The present author strongly advises against the specific method he is considering. The present author is aware this advice will not be solicited and will not be heeded.
title: Venice, Italy
description: Venice in February 1817 is the most magically active location on the continent outside England, because England's most brilliant and most reckless magician is here, using its mirrors for experiments that are blurring the boundary between the mortal world and Faerie. The city is a mirror-maze by architecture. Strange has not gone mad yet. He is working on it.

keys: Starecross Hall Yorkshire , Segundus's school ,
value:
Starecross Hall, Yorkshire. [See also: Lady Pole, John Segundus, Starecross Hall — The Captive Speaks in Patterns, Changeling Returned, Mr. Honeyfoot.]
A former country house converted by John Segundus into a school for young ladies — and, secretly, a sanctuary for Lady Pole. Lady Pole spends her days in increasing agitation, unable to speak directly of her captivity; when she tries, fairy tales erupt from her lips instead. Segundus has discovered that those tales follow a sequence — and that mapped together, they describe the interior of Lost-hope, including the location of the Raven King's throne. She is smuggling intelligence through the enchantment's restrictions. Segundus has no great power. He has supernatural sensitivity, moral courage, and nine years of evidence accumulating. Mr. Honeyfoot visits regularly from London, providing practical support. Breaking Lady Pole's enchantment requires the restoration of her severed finger, currently in a box held by Lascelles. The relay that will eventually bring it to Starecross has not yet begun.
¹ The enchantment was designed to prevent Lady Pole from communicating her situation. It did not account for a captive with nine years to study the enchantment's aesthetic register and find what it could not suppress. Starecross is, in the present author's view, the most important address in England after Hurtfew Abbey, and for entirely opposite reasons.
title: Starecross Hall, Yorkshire
description: A quiet school in Yorkshire that is secretly the most important magical intelligence hub in England. Lady Pole has been held captive at Lost-hope every night for nine years. She cannot speak of her captivity directly. She has found another way. John Segundus is decoding it, and what she is communicating, in pieces, through the fairy tales the enchantment forces her to tell, includes a description of Lost-hope's interior.

keys: The King's Roads , the mirror roads ,
value:
The King's Roads. [See also: The King's Roads, Faerie's Geography, The Lantern-Carrier, The King's Roads — The Crossroads Collapse, The King's Roads Encounter Template.]
A vast network of paths through the space between mirrors — accessible through any reflective surface, traversing a landscape of grey twilight that is not quite earth under foot or sky above. They were once the highways of John Uskglass's triple kingdom. They are now reopening, volatile and incompletely mapped. Three categories of inhabitant: the Lost (travelers who took a wrong turn days, decades, or centuries ago — each category presenting differently, each requiring different response); the Things at the Margins (displaced from the space before the Roads were built through it, calling in familiar voices, following at exactly the same distance regardless of pace); and the Gentleman's Traces (near the Lost-hope junction, strands of dance music with no source, the faint smell of a ballroom). The Lantern-Carrier — a child-sized figure with a cold blue lantern — indicates safe routes. Its guidance is more reliable than any alternative. Travelers who follow it arrive at their destination. Travelers who ignore it become lost in the specific category of lost that has more variety to it than ordinary lost.
¹ The Sídhe and Mélusine road-networks have overlapped at a previously stable intersection. Travelers entering from England are emerging in Brittany. Both courts blame the other. See The Crossroads Collapse.
title: The King's Roads
description: The fairy road network beneath all mirror surfaces — a grey twilight between reflective surfaces that connects arbitrary points across Europe and Faerie simultaneously. Once the highways of the ancient Raven King, now reopening and volatile. Instant travel at the cost of fairy-bargain risk. Three types of inhabitants, none entirely safe. The Lantern-Carrier will show you the way, if you follow it.

keys: The Scholomance Transylvanian Alps , the Devil's underground school ,
value:
The Scholomance, Transylvanian Alps. [See also: Balkan Dragon-Scholar Magic, The Schoolmaster, The Scholomance — The Tenth Student, Gheorghe the Doorkeeper, Andrei Stormwalker.]
An underground school in the Carpathian Mountains where the Schoolmaster — a Fae lord of considerable power and no particular empathy — teaches nine students for seven years and retains the tenth permanently. It has reopened as of 1817. The entrance moves: a crack in different cliff-faces, a cave mouth that was not there yesterday, a door in a mountainside that opens only to those who bear the birth-signs. Gheorghe the Doorkeeper — the Weathermaker of his graduating class, who has sat at the entrance for decades — administers the admission test: three questions about the nature of weather. Those who answer correctly find the cave mouth opening. The current class is approaching graduation. The tenth student has sent a message from within, begging rescue, tucked inside a hailstone. The Schoolmaster considers initiative a promising quality. It will not change his accounting. Andrei Stormwalker, a graduate, rides storms across the Balkans and tests hospitality as a tall, red-haired, white-robed beggar.
¹ The Scholomance's gates are currently locked. A graduate returned raving, his dragon dead. See The Scholomance — A Graduate Returns. The Librarian is pulling texts that predate the school's founding. The Schoolmaster characterises his response as scholarly caution.
title: The Scholomance, Transylvanian Alps
description: The underground school where a Fae lord teaches weather magic and dragon-riding to nine students, keeps the tenth permanently, and considers this arrangement entirely reasonable. It has reopened. The current class is almost finished. One student has smuggled out a message begging rescue. The gates are currently locked following a graduate's alarming return. This should be taken as information.

keys: The Fairy Mounds Ireland , the Irish hollow hills ,
value:
The Fairy Mounds, Ireland. [See also: Celtic Otherworld Magic, Brigid Ní Fhlaithbheartaigh, The Fairy Mounds — The Open Door, The Sídhe Court, Finvarra.]
Thousands of earthen mounds, ring forts, and raths scattered across the Irish landscape — each one an entrance to the dwelling-places of the Sídhe. The great mounds include Brú na Bóinne (Newgrange), Knockmaa Hill (Finvarra's seat), and the Hill of Tara. Brú na Bóinne has opened — its entrance, normally admitting sunlight only at the winter solstice, is now permanently illuminated by cold light from within. People are walking in and not walking out. Brigid Ní Fhlaithbheartaigh stands at the entrance turning back everyone she can, but the light is calling some people by name, and those people cannot resist it. Cián of the Silver Tongue, Finvarra's courtier, was asked about this. His response was courteous, detailed, and upon careful reading, legally meaningless. The Sídhe Court has not sent a representative to the entrance. It does not consider an open door to require explanation.
¹ The fairy tithe is approaching — seven years since the last mortal sacrifice. Finvarra is selecting which captive will be given. Rumour within the court suggests the chosen is someone whose sacrifice will have political consequences in the mortal world.
title: The Fairy Mounds, Ireland
description: Thousands of fairy mounds dot every parish in Ireland — each one an entrance to the Sídhe Court beneath. The great mound at Newgrange has opened permanently, its interior lit by cold fairy light. People are walking in. Ireland's fairy doctors are the most effective practitioners for navigating Sídhe territory. The fairy tithe approaches. The court is selecting its sacrifice.

keys: Brocken Peak Harz Mountains , the Brocken ,
value:
Brocken Peak, Harz Mountains. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, Magister Albrecht Krone, Brocken Peak — Walpurgisnacht in February, The Æsir Court, The One-Eyed Lord.]
The highest peak in northern Germany — legendary Walpurgisnacht gathering site, seasonal Æsir Court meeting ground, and intersection of several Fae territorial boundaries making it a neutral but volatile location. The Brocken spectre — a climber's shadow projected enormous on clouds below, surrounded by a rainbow halo — is a genuine magical effect. Magister Albrecht Krone has been at the summit shepherd's hut for six months, filling seven notebooks with observations of Æsir Court Fae activity. He has no magical ability whatsoever and has survived through iron charms, meticulous observational protocols, and what he suspects is the Fae's genuine amusement at being studied. An unscheduled Æsir Court assembly is currently ongoing — three months early, in February rather than May. Krone's latest notebook entry: 'They are arguing about something. They are afraid.' Something has frightened a court that prizes courage above all qualities and considers fear aesthetically disqualifying.
¹ A new rune has appeared on Yggdrasil that the One-Eyed Lord did not carve. The assembly and the rune are connected. The connection has not been explained to any mortal.
title: Brocken Peak, Harz Mountains
description: The legendary Walpurgisnacht site and the Æsir Court's seasonal meeting ground is currently hosting an unscheduled assembly. Something has frightened the Norse Fae court. A natural philosopher has been at the summit for six months with no magical ability, filling notebooks by surviving on iron charms and the Fae's amusement at being observed scientifically. The Brocken spectre is real.

keys: The Stones of Carnac Brittany , the Breton standing stones ,
value:
The Stones of Carnac, Brittany. [See also: French Grimoire Magic, Yannick le Braz, The Stones of Carnac — The Humming, The Sídhe Court, The Mélusine Court.]
Over three thousand megalithic standing stones arranged in rows stretching for miles along the Breton coast — the largest such arrangement in Europe. They are humming. The tone is not quite music and not quite language; it is felt in the bones as much as heard with the ears, and its frequency is rising. The korrigans — Breton fairy beings who inhabit the megalithic landscape — are dancing more frequently and more violently among the stones and will not explain why. Yannick le Braz, a fisherman's son born in a caul, is the only person the korrigans will speak to; he charges the Paris scholars converging on the site a fee he considers fair and the scholars find impertinent. He charges the korrigans as well, in cream and honey. The Mélusine Court has sent a notary to investigate, which reveals they did not expect this. The stones predate both courts' claims to Brittany. Whatever they respond to is older than either court's paperwork.
¹ The Crossroads Collapse on the King's Roads — the Sídhe and Mélusine road-networks overlapping in the strait between Britain and France — may be connected to the humming. The Chief Notary has opened a new file. Its title is not yet determined.
title: The Stones of Carnac, Brittany
description: Three thousand standing stones arranged in miles of rows along the Breton coast, currently producing an audible harmonic tone felt in the bones. The korrigans are agitated and will not say why. Scholars from across Europe are arriving. The Mélusine Court's notary has arrived to investigate, which reveals the Court was surprised. The stones predate every Fae court's claim to Brittany.

keys: Lake Snagov and the Carpathian Passes , the Wallachian passes ,
value:
Lake Snagov and the Carpathian Passes, Romania. [See also: Balkan Dragon-Scholar Magic, Elena Barbu, Lake Snagov — The Strigoi Procession, The Scholomance Court, Andrei Stormwalker.]
The most magically saturated mountain range in Europe — the Scholomance Court's heartland, riddled with strigoi (both living and dead), haunted by iele (irresistibly beautiful nocturnal fairy women whose attention is catastrophic), and patrolled by zmeu (dragon-beings of human intelligence). The mountain passes are controlled by supernatural forces as much as political borders; travelers report toll-collectors demanding payment in memories, names, or years of life. Lake Snagov, surrounded by ancient forest, sits on the boundary between the living world and the dead world — the strigoi morți have emerged from the forest and are marching in organised formation toward an unknown destination, ignoring Elena Barbu's descântece entirely, which has never happened before. She has sent word to Andrei Stormwalker. She has not received a reply.
¹ The Scholomance's gates are currently locked after a graduate returned raving from the Carpathian passes, describing something that is neither mortal, Fae, dead, nor alive. The Librarian is pulling texts. Khalid ibn Rashid, when asked, said the Djinn have a word for what it sounds like and advised extreme caution.
title: Lake Snagov and the Carpathian Passes
description: The most supernaturally dense landscape in Europe — the Scholomance Court's heartland, where witch, vampire, dragon, and fairy categories blur together and the boundary between the living world and the dead is perilously thin. The walking dead are marching in organised formation. The mountain passes demand payment in memories. The Scholomance's gates are locked. Something was found in the passes that the Schoolmaster has not yet characterised.

keys: Zugarramurdi Cave Basque Country , the cave of the Basque witches ,
value:
Zugarramurdi Cave, Basque Country. [See also: Iberian Blood-and-Stone Magic, Aitor Etxeberria, Zugarramurdi Cave — Mari Speaks, The Mari Court, Maddi.]
The cave where the great akelarre was held — site of the 1610 Inquisition trial that sent eleven to death. Infernuko Erreka ('Hell's Stream') runs at its mouth. The cave's vast natural interior contains an entrance to the Mari Court. Mari has spoken directly from every cave mouth in the Pyrenees simultaneously — one sentence in Euskara ('The old compact is broken. New terms are required') and one in a language no living person recognises. No one has established what the old compact was. Aitor Etxeberria — twenty years old, hearing Mari's voice from this cave since he was fifteen, following his grandmother's instructions for protocol — lights candles in the stream and relays messages between the Mari Court and the villages. He has not entered the cave. His grandmother forbade it until Mari came to collect him. He prays to both Mari and the Virgin Mary, having decided the theological question is above his competence and that thoroughness is the safer approach. Maddi, Mari's twenty-one-year-old voice to the mortal world, says Mari is waiting for something.
¹ The Inquisition has been prosecuting sorginak since the Zugarramurdi trials of 1610. The sorginak have continued practicing. The Inquisition's record in this exchange is not, by the present author's assessment, strong.
title: Zugarramurdi Cave, Basque Country
description: The cave where the Inquisition held its most famous witchcraft trial in 1610. An entrance to the oldest Fae court in Western Europe. Mari has just spoken publicly from every cave mouth in the Pyrenees simultaneously, announcing that an old compact has been broken and new terms are required. No one knows what the compact was. The Inquisition's records of the 1610 trial remain open.

keys: The Sacred Groves of Samogitia Lithuania , the rekindled Lithuanian groves ,
value:
The Sacred Groves of Samogitia, Lithuania. [See also: Baltic Sacred Fire Magic, Dalia Kazlauskienė, Lithuania — The Fire Spreads, The Romuva Court, Vytautas the Flame-Carrier.]
The last pagan sacred groves in Europe — sites where eternal fires were maintained by priests until Christian suppression in the early fifteenth century. Dalia Kazlauskienė, a grandmother of approximately seventy, was walking in the forest when she found a fire burning in a clearing her grandmother had described in stories four generations old: the sacred clearing of a Romuva grove, untended for four hundred years, suddenly lit again with a fire no one had set. She sat down. She fed it oak branches. She has been there since. Six additional daughter-fires have appeared at grove sites across Samogitia. Vytautas the Flame-Carrier moves between them with his sacred lantern, carrying messages from Perkūnas. Russian authorities have dispatched a military detachment. Juozas Vaitkevičius is trying to reach the groves before the soldiers with notebooks documenting that the fires are benevolent. The military detachment's instructions do not specify what to do upon encountering a fire that burns without fuel.
¹ Žaltvykslė — a žaltys of unusual size, coiled at the base of the central oak — has been accepting milk from Dalia every morning since the fire returned. She accepts it as her due, which it is.
title: The Sacred Groves of Samogitia, Lithuania
description: The last sacred groves of the last pagan nation in Europe, where eternal fires are rekindling spontaneously at sites untended since 1387. The Romuva Court barely had time to sleep. A grandmother who remembered the old stories in four-generation whispers is now tending the fire. The thunder-being has appeared at the grove for the first time since magic's return. The Russian military is on its way.

keys: Elsinore and the Danish Straits , Kronborg Castle ,
value:
Elsinore and the Danish Straits. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, Kapitajn Søren Madsen, Elsinore — Something Wakes, The Æsir Court, Eiríkr the Walker.]
Kronborg Castle guards the narrow strait between Denmark and Sweden — and, it is now apparent, a maritime gate between the mortal world and the Æsir Court's oceanic domain. Kapitajn Madsen has filed reports for eighteen months about vibrations cracking masonry, fish dying in the strait, water turning deep unnatural blue. His logbook: 'Whatever sleeps beneath us turned over in its sleep last night. Two sentries fainted. The castle dog will not stop howling.' The Danish government is sending an investigation. They do not know what they are investigating. Eiríkr the Walker — the One-Eyed Lord's ground-level observer, a conscious draugr who has been dead for eight hundred years — has been observed near the castle twice this month without speaking to anyone. He walks with the patience of a man who has already seen whatever is about to happen.
¹ The present author earmarks: What Sleeps at Elsinore. See the Lore entry. The connection between the unknown rune on Yggdrasil, the Æsir Court's unscheduled assembly on the Brocken, the draugr rising across Scandinavia, and the Elsinore vibrations has not been publicly established. Eiríkr the Walker has not confirmed it. He has been observing it.
title: Elsinore and the Danish Straits
description: Kronborg Castle guards the narrow strait between Denmark and Sweden — and a gate between the mortal world and something that has been sleeping beneath it for a very long time. The vibrations are cracking masonry. The water has turned an unnatural blue. The Æsir Court's ground-level observer has been seen near the castle twice this month and has not spoken to anyone.

keys: The Pindus Mountains Greece , the Greek highlands ,
value:
The Pindus Mountains, Greece. [See also: Mediterranean Eye-Ward Magic, Kyriakos Papadimitriou, The Pindus Mountains — The Neraides' Price, The Olympian Court, Hermes-Who-Was.]
The mountainous spine of Greece, home to the neraides (descended from ancient nymphs) and the last strongholds of folk practices stretching back to classical antiquity. Remote mountain villages preserve rituals continuous since the Byzantine era. The Filiki Eteria — the secret society plotting Greek independence — has discovered that the mountain spirits answer Greek prayers and hinder Ottoman patrols. Kyriakos Papadimitriou commands a klepht band of freedom fighters who have been receiving supernatural assistance from the neraides for two years without having asked for it. The neraides have now named their price: a mortal man must marry into their community permanently. One of Kyriakos's fighters has volunteered. The neraides say this is not a request. The term they used for what it is translates approximately as 'a statement of terms already agreed to, which you have perhaps forgotten, and which we have not.'
¹ The Olympian Court is backing Greek independence — not from concern for sovereignty but because a free Greece might restore their cultural centrality. The Grey-Eyed Counselor has presented terms to the Filiki Eteria that the leadership finds horrifying and the mountain fighters are becoming too dependent on supernatural assistance to refuse.
title: The Pindus Mountains, Greece
description: The mountainous spine of Greece, where the Olympian Court is active, where the neraides guide Greek freedom fighters through passes that Ottoman patrols cannot navigate, and where a revolutionary conspiracy is discovering that supernatural assistance comes with supernatural terms. The old gods are backing Greek independence. The price is becoming clear.

keys: Ashfair Shropshire , Strange's Shropshire seat ,
value:
Ashfair, Shropshire. [See also: English Practical Magic, Jonathan Strange, Ashfair — The Grave That Grows, Genius Loci, The Moss-Oak Fetch.]
Strange's family seat — the place where the moss-oak fetch arrived, behaved wrongly for three days, and died. A piece of Faerie made from ancient bog-preserved wood and animated by fairy magic is buried in English soil under Arabella Strange's name. The grave is doing something unusual: moss spreads from it in concentric rings, an old yew's roots have bent toward the plot as if drawn, the grass over it moves in a direction unrelated to the wind. The genius loci of Ashfair are disturbed — the land-spirits witnessed what arrived and what it was. They know the difference between a woman and a log. They have always known. No one has thought to ask them yet. A practitioner with the right training might communicate with them; their testimony would constitute the first reliable evidence that Arabella Strange is alive rather than dead. Strange is in Venice. He does not know about the grave's behaviour. The vicar has written to the Bishop. The Bishop has not replied.
¹ Arabella Strange has spent months in Lost-hope observing the Gentleman's court, its rules, its protocols, and its weaknesses. She is not passive. A practitioner who reaches Lost-hope should seek her counsel before attempting any negotiation with the Gentleman.
title: Ashfair, Shropshire
description: Strange's family home in Shropshire, where his wife was falsely buried — the grave contains a fairy-made wooden duplicate that died. The grave is doing something unusual. The spirits of the English landscape witnessed what arrived and know the difference between a woman and a log. No one has thought to ask them what they saw. This is an oversight.

keys: The Singing Stone of Karelia Finland , the Karelian rune-stone ,
value:
The Singing Stone of Karelia, Finland. [See also: Finno-Ugric Origin-Song Magic, Old Matti Karjalainen, The Singing Stone of Karelia — The Origin Deepens, The Väki Court, The Singing Void.]
A glacial erratic in the forests of eastern Finland — a boulder of grey granite deposited by ice ten thousand years ago — that has begun producing audible origin-charms. The sound is a vibration felt in the chest, resolving into words only for those trained in the tietäjä tradition. Runo-singers from across Karelia are converging on the stone. Old Matti Karjalainen — approximately eighty, carrying song-fragments no other living person remembers — has been sitting beside it for three weeks and has stopped eating, though he does not appear to be losing weight. The sound has shifted from the origin of stone to the origin of origins: a recursive incantation describing the moment before the first thing existed. Runo-singers who listen too long lose the ability to perceive individual objects — everything dissolves into its component origins. This is Finland's primary magical locus: the Väki Court's closest approach to physical expression, the Singing Void expressed in granite.
¹ Elias Lönnrot is currently fourteen years old and has not yet met Old Matti Karjalainen. This is expected to change. The oral tradition that Lönnrot will eventually compile as the Kalevala is extant and active. Old Matti is its primary living repository.
title: The Singing Stone of Karelia, Finland
description: A glacial boulder in eastern Finland that has begun producing origin-charms audible only to Finnish magical practitioners. The Väki Court's closest approach to physical expression. Runo-singers from across Karelia are converging on it. The oldest living practitioner is sitting beside it and has stopped eating. The stone's origin-songs are recursive — currently describing the moment before the first thing existed.

keys: Harley Street and Hanover Square London , Sir Walter's London house ,
value:
Harley Street and Hanover Square, London. [See also: English Practical Magic, Stephen Black, Lost-hope — The Ball That Won't End, The Raven King's Court, Sir Walter Pole.]
The two London addresses that anchor the novel's English storyline. Sir Walter Pole's house in Harley Street is where Stephen Black serves as butler by day — maintaining impeccable professional composure — and attends the Gentleman's compulsory balls at Lost-hope by night, unable to speak of his captivity. The Gentleman's face has been glimpsed in the house mirrors. Stephen carries the Raven King's ancient alliances with England's natural forces without knowing it: trees, water, stones, shadows answer him. Sir Walter knows something is terribly wrong with his wife Emma and cannot identify what. Norrell's house in Hanover Square is his London base, managed by Lascelles and Drawlight, where The Friends of English Magic is produced and the Norrellite faction conducts its political operations. Lascelles is cold, intelligent, and genuinely dangerous. Drawlight is vain, shallow, and susceptible to flattery. Between these two houses, the English magical crisis is being quietly enacted.
¹ Mrs. Lennox, Norrell's housekeeper at Hurtfew and London, identifies rooms where magic has been performed by a faint smell of lightning. She has a more complete picture of operations than Norrell has elected to share. She will not give access without a reason that satisfies her, which is a higher bar than satisfying Norrell.
title: Harley Street and Hanover Square, London
description: The two London addresses at the centre of the English magical crisis: Harley Street, where Stephen Black endures nightly fairy enchantment while maintaining perfect professional composure, and Hanover Square, where Norrell's institutional magic is managed by increasingly dangerous gatekeepers. The Gentleman's face appears in the Harley Street mirrors. The Thames is paying attention.

keys: Lost-hope , the other court ,
value:
Lost-hope (The Raven King's Shadow Court). [See also: The Raven King's Court, The Gentleman with the Thistle-Down Hair, Lady Pole, Stephen Black, Lost-hope — The Empty Throne Glows.]
Accessible through mirrors and the King's Roads — a crumbling palace of impossible beauty where enchanted mortals dance in gorgeous misery, every ballroom beautiful and slightly wrong, every flower without scent, every fairy light illuminating nothing useful. The Gentleman occupies it as a squatter in the Raven King's absence. Deep inside, past the ballrooms, a chamber the Gentleman never enters contains a throne of black stone carved with ravens that has begun to glow. The Gentleman has noticed and is pretending not to find this alarming. He considers the throne's former owner 'a rather excellent predecessor.' He has not sat in the chair. He is aware it would not receive him. Lady Pole and Stephen Black are held here every night against their will. Arabella Strange is captive here, observing everything.
¹ The present author notes that the most useful intelligence about Lost-hope's interior is currently held by Lady Pole, who is encoding it in fairy tales at Starecross. The second most useful is held by Arabella Strange, who has had considerably more time to observe and has been planning an escape she cannot yet execute.
title: Lost-hope

keys: The Hollow Hills , the hills of the other country ,
value:
The Hollow Hills (The Sídhe Court). [See also: The Sídhe Court, Finvarra, Oonagh, Cián of the Silver Tongue, The Hollow Hills — The Tithe Approaches.]
The Sídhe Court exists beneath thousands of earthen mounds and fairy forts across Ireland, Scotland, Wales, and Brittany, its true seat at Brú na Bóinne — a passage tomb older than the pyramids. The court operates on fairy time: an hour inside may be a year without. Finvarra, High King of the Connacht Sídhe, governs through elaborate feudal protocol at Knockmaa Hill. His queen Oonagh determines changeling policy and oversees the fairy tithe. The Sídhe are the most politically sophisticated Fae court in Europe. They do not rage; they wait. They do not conquer; they acquire. Brú na Bóinne has opened and people are walking in. The tithe is approaching. Finvarra is selecting the mortal who will be given.
¹ Entry without invitation or a fairy doctor's guidance is strongly inadvisable. Entry with Brigid Ní Fhlaithbheartaigh's guidance is merely dangerous. These are not the same category.
title: The Hollow Hills

keys: Valhöll and the Halls of the World Tree , the Norse hall of the slain ,
value:
Valhöll and the Halls of the World Tree (The Æsir Court). [See also: The Æsir Court, The One-Eyed Lord, Huginn, Muninn, Sigríður Kolsdóttir, Valhöll — A Rune Appears.]
Distributed across the Nine Realms connected by Yggdrasil. Mortal-world access at Brocken Peak, Icelandic volcanic sites, and ancient ritual grounds. The court's hall is vast, lit by firelight, echoing with ravens. The One-Eyed Lord presides as curator of interesting fates — he collects knowledge with a possessive mania that mortals mistook for wisdom. A new rune has appeared on Yggdrasil that he did not carve and does not recognise. He has sent the ravens to observe it from every angle. Their report has not resolved his uncertainty, which is a condition he has not experienced in several centuries. Sigríður Kolsdóttir guards the threshold — a dead völva in the One-Eyed Lord's service who tests visitors with riddles and strikes those who answer with contempt, with lightning she finds regrettable rather than pleasurable.
¹ Approaching the Æsir Court requires either very specific bargaining skill or the willingness to hang from a tree for nine nights and see what becomes interesting. The present author does not recommend the latter without considerable preparation and a clear sense of what you intend to learn.
title: Valhöll and the Halls of the World Tree

keys: Tuonela and the Forest Beyond , the Finnish land of the dead ,
value:
Tuonela and the Forest Beyond (The Väki Court). [See also: The Väki Court, The Singing Void, The Echo-Child, Tuonela — The River Thins, Ahti.]
The Väki Court exists in the spaces between — sauna steam, the origin-moment of fire, the silence at the centre of a Finnish forest where sound stops carrying. Tuonela, the Finnish underworld, is reached by crossing a dark river that is currently running shallow: now wadeable at certain points by ordinary mortals, with the dead visible on the near bank. The Echo-Child stands on the far bank, repeating visitors' words with slight alterations that reveal hidden intentions — recently shifted to revealing hidden outcomes. The Singing Void is audible at the near bank for the first time. It has never previously been audible above ground. The Väki Court has no throne because it needs none. Knowledge is the only hierarchy. The price of everything here is epistemic.
¹ Tietäjät who have visited Tuonela in the past month report it is louder than it has ever been. The question 'what is the Väki Court producing?' presupposes the Väki Court distinguishes production from the natural state of things, which it may not.
title: Tuonela and the Forest Beyond

keys: The Island of Buyan , the Slavic spirit island ,
value:
The Island of Buyan (The Parliament of Domains). [See also: The Parliament of Domains, Dedushka Petr, The Wandering Domovoi, Buyan — The Parliament Deadlocks, Zagovory.]
Buyan rises from a sacred ocean, centred on the stone Alatyr from which all zagovory draw power. The Parliament convenes here — the great spirits as first among equals, thousands of domain-spirits each constitutionally incapable of acknowledging any domain but their own. Dedushka Petr, a domovoi of ancient and emphatic opinions in a red shirt without shoes, presides at the threshold. He requires evidence of proper courtesy to one's own household domovoi before he admits visitors to Buyan. He can be mollified with bread and salt and genuine courtesy — genuine being operative, as household spirits communicate and will know. The Parliament is currently deadlocked over the Białowieża border dispute. This is not unusual. The stakes have not previously been this high. Dedushka Petr has stopped offering hospitality to visitors.
¹ The Wandering Domovoi — a domovoi whose family emigrated to America, leaving him technically homeless — carries diplomatic correspondence between the Parliament's scattered representatives. He can indicate the junction on the King's Roads that leads to Buyan. He charges for this information in interesting gossip.
title: The Island of Buyan

keys: The Sacred Grove of Romuva , the Lithuanian sacred hearth ,
value:
The Sacred Grove of Romuva (The Romuva Court). [See also: The Romuva Court, The Thunderer of the Sacred Oak, Laima, Žaltvykslė, Romuva Grove — The Fire Changes Color.]
A physical grove in the forests of Samogitia, Lithuania, where the eternal flame has rekindled. The Romuva Court's seat has reignited — the youngest Fae court in Europe, barely four centuries suppressed, fully awake. The fire has turned blue. Dalia Kazlauskienė tends it. The Thunderer of the Sacred Oak is present at the grove for the first time since magic's return, standing before the blue flame and saying nothing. In the Romuva tradition, when he says nothing, this is itself a statement. Žaltvykslė — a sacred grass snake of unusual size — coils at the base of the central oak. She accepts milk every morning as her due. Laima, who determines every living being's fate, appears at the grove with the composure of a being who already knows how every conversation ends — which she does.
¹ Giltinė — Laima's sister, the death-collector — has been observed near the grove three times in the past month. She has not collected anyone. She appears to be waiting. In Romuva tradition, Giltinė waiting is indistinguishable from Giltinė arriving at a moment not yet reached.
title: The Sacred Grove of Romuva

keys: The Cave Beneath Olympus , the cave below Olympus ,
value:
The Cave Beneath Olympus (The Olympian Court). [See also: The Olympian Court, The Lord of the Shining Mountain, The Grey-Eyed Counselor, Hermes-Who-Was, Cave Beneath Olympus — The Lord of the Shining Mountain Weeps.]
Not the summit but vast caverns of white marble and running water within the mountain, lit by light that is too perfect and too warm to be real — an idealised Mediterranean landscape reconstructed from nostalgic memory. The beings mortals called Zeus, Athena, and Hermes are Fae of tremendous age and wounded pride. The Lord of the Shining Mountain is currently weeping; thunderstorms have wracked Greece for a week. Something has been communicated to him that has broken his composure. The Grey-Eyed Counselor is investigating. Her expression, rarely unguarded, is. Hermes-Who-Was — who calls himself the Threshold-Crosser, has winged sandals that may or may not be literal, and is the only Olympian who genuinely enjoys mortal company — is the court's most accessible entry point.
¹ Hermes-Who-Was will guide you through the caves below Olympus. He has never claimed he would warn you about what is inside. The distinction between 'accessible' and 'safe' is one the present author recommends keeping clearly in mind.
title: The Cave Beneath Olympus

keys: The Cave of Mari , the Basque mountain cave ,
value:
The Cave of Mari (The Mari Court). [See also: The Mari Court, Mari, Sugaar, The Lamia of Mundaka, Cave of Mari — The Seasonal Migration Fails.]
Mari's seat is the cave system of Anboto in the Basque mountains — migrating seasonally between Anboto, Aketegi, Txindoki, and other peaks according to a cycle that controls the weather. The seasonal migration has stalled. Mari remains at Anboto; the Pyrenean weather is in disarray. She is waiting for something, which is a condition the tradition has no name for. The cave approaches the geological rather than the architectural: not carved or decorated but grown, with mineral formations that pulse with slow geological breath. There is no light that doesn't come from the stone itself. The Lamia of Mundaka — one of the lamiak, river-fairies of the Basque country — serves as the Mari Court's most accessible intermediary, receiving visitors at riverside rocks at dusk with a golden comb and accepting tribute. The golden comb is the most reliable tribute. Do not remark on the bird-feet.
¹ Mari cannot be deceived. Intent, not wording. Practitioners who approach with concealed motives find the concealed portion extracted as payment. The only protection is complete honesty before a geological Fae who controls the weather. This is its own form of courage.
title: The Cave of Mari

keys: The Grimoire Vault , the fairy library beneath Paris ,
value:
The Grimoire Vault (The Mélusine Court). [See also: The Mélusine Court, The Chief Notary, Mélusine, Maître Corveau, Paris — The Catacomb Breach.]
Accessible through specific medieval cellars in Poitou and certain passages in the Paris catacombs — though since the catacomb breach, an unsanctioned entry point now exists beneath a bookshop on the Quai des Grands-Augustins. The court itself: an underground library-palace of vaulted stone, endless shelves, and reading desks where fairy scribes copy texts in handwriting that shifts when you are not looking. Everything is catalogued. The beauty is administrative. Maître Corveau manages initial consultations — thin, in sixteenth-century clerk's clothing, professionally neutral, helpful and condescending in proportions so balanced they cancel into neutrality. He explains the vault's rules. No documents to be removed. No documents to be damaged. No documents read aloud without prior authorisation. Words have power here; the last item is the one that matters. The Chief Notary is the entity you actually negotiate with: thin, pale, ink-stained, possessed of a patience that is geological. They remember every term of every agreement ever negotiated in this vault.
¹ Mélusine herself — the serpent-fairy founder — may or may not still preside. Her shadow is sometimes seen in the deepest passages. The village of Lusignan bakes cakes called mères lusines on certain feast days. Those who eat them near the castle report dreams of a tall woman examining documents with great care.
title: The Grimoire Vault

keys: The Springs of Solomon , the Djinn's Jordan Valley seat ,
value:
The Springs of Solomon (The Djinn Courts). [See also: The Djinn Courts, The Ifrit of the Pillars, Khalid ibn Rashid, Zahra bint Yusuf, The Levant — The Debts Come Due.]
A network of geothermal springs across the Jordan Valley and Dead Sea basin where the oldest Djinn convene. Mortal practitioners approach at sunset and wait to be noticed. Being noticed is not guaranteed. Being noticed and found unworthy is dangerous. Khalid ibn Rashid maintains a network of contacts at the major spring-sites who can advise on protocol — the first rule being: do not approach the water until invited. The second rule being: the invitation may not come in words. The Djinn spaces feel like standing inside a mathematical proof written in fire: angular, precise, blindingly luminous, everything crystalline and exact. The Ifrit of the Pillars — the Congregation's most senior member, a column of heat-shimmer that occasionally resolves into a face — has been accounting for eight centuries of compounded debt and is not in a generous mood. It is, however, in an informative one.
¹ Zahra bint Yusuf in Cairo is reconstructing the Solomonic binding seals from fragmented family copies. The intact form — sufficient for permanent constraint — appears lost. Practitioners who approach the Springs without adequate binding capacity are not practitioners. They are offerings.
title: The Springs of Solomon

keys: The Narcissist's Heaven , the bright country of the Narcissist ,
value:
The Narcissist's Heaven (The King's Roads). [See also: The Narcissist, Brother Ezekiel Thorne, The King's Roads — The Light Grows Brighter, Abrahamic Fringe Magic.]
A section of the King's Roads blazing with self-generated light — the only portion of the grey twilight network that is not grey. The Narcissist has occupied this space since before the Raven King built his Roads through it; the Roads were routed around this squatter who believes it owns the entire dimension. The space contains no architecture, only light and the distant sound of singing. Mortal souls collected over millennia reside here in a state the Narcissist calls paradise and that visitors from other courts describe as 'a very bright room full of very tired people.' The zone is expanding — Tambora's famine has driven millions to prayer across Europe, and prayer is its sustenance. Practitioners following Abrahamic traditions may find themselves drawn toward this light when on the Roads. The pull is real. The light is genuine. Brother Ezekiel Thorne in Shoreditch is receiving what he believes are divine revelations with increasing frequency and specificity. He is correct that the source is real. He has not yet established what the source is.
¹ The Narcissist does not acknowledge the existence of other courts. It does not know the name 'Narcissist' exists. This would require acknowledging the existence of beings who coined it.
title: The Narcissist's Heaven

keys: Fae Courts: Universal Principles , the properties common to all Fae ,
value:
Fae Courts: Universal Principles. [See also: Fairy Debt, Iron Vulnerability, Faerie's Geography, The Bargain.]
Every Fae court in Europe shares certain properties, regardless of tradition or antiquity. The present author sets them out once and will not repeat them in individual entries.
Fae cannot lie. This is not a virtue. They deceive absolutely through selective truth, misleading emphasis, and the exploitation of every ambiguity the mortal failed to close. A Fae who says 'I will not harm you' may arrange for something else to do so. Reciprocity governs all dealings: gifts create obligations, debts accrue interest, and a Fae who owes a mortal is as genuinely bound as the reverse. Cold iron wounds or repels every court without exception — degree varies, but immunity does not exist. Fae realms operate on their own temporal logic; a mortal cannot predict how much time will pass outside while inside. A Fae's true name grants power over it; this is why they are addressed by euphemism everywhere in Europe. Every Fae court's seat exists in Faerie — a dimension separate from the mortal world. The locations described as access points are where the boundary thins. Two entrances in different countries may open into neighbouring Fae kingdoms; Faerie's geography bears no relation to the mortal world's.
¹ This document earmarks further inter-court dynamics to the Lore entry 'Inter-Court Politics.' The present author suggests reading that entry before attempting negotiations with more than one court simultaneously.
title: Fae Courts: Universal Principles

keys: The Raven King's Court , the other court of England ,
value:
The Raven King's Court. [See also: John Uskglass, The Gentleman with the Thistle-Down Hair, Lost-Hope, Genius Loci, The King's Roads, Stephen Black, Lady Pole.]
The court is officially empty. John Uskglass departed in 1434 and has not returned — though in February 1817, his throne at Lost-hope has begun to glow, which the Gentleman has noticed and is pretending not to find alarming. Lost-hope is accessible through mirrors and the King's Roads. It is a crumbling palace of impossible beauty: ballrooms where enchanted mortals dance in gorgeous misery, gardens of flowers with no scent. The Gentleman who currently occupies it is a Fae of moderate power and limitless vanity who has mistaken squatting for sovereignty. He collects beautiful and interesting mortals for his amusement, enchants them into attendance at nightly balls, and is genuinely incapable of understanding why they object. He is a coward when genuinely threatened. The deeper court — England's genius loci, the spirits of stone and rain and river bound by the Raven King's ancient treaties — answers to no one at present. It waits. Strange and Norrell are the spell it has been waiting for.
¹ The Gentleman's bargains exploit the gap between what a mortal assumes and what words actually mean. He offered a magician 'half' a woman's life. He took her nights. Both parties agreed. Both parties understood different things. The contract was honoured exactly.
² Mortal euphemisms: 'His Court,' 'the Other Court,' 'persons of the other world.' The Gentleman is never named aloud.
title: The Raven King's Court

keys: The Sídhe Court , the court of the Good People ,
value:
The Sídhe Court. [See also: Celtic Otherworld Magic, Finvarra, Oonagh, Gwyn ap Nudd, The Geis, Fairy Tithe.]
Accessible from the Hollow Hills — primarily Brú na Bóinne and Knockmaa in Ireland, the Hill of Tara, and hundreds of lesser fairy forts across the Celtic lands; the Welsh branch through Annwn. In Faerie, the Sídhe Court is a vast kingdom of ageless twilight bearing no relation to the mortal hills above it. The court is the most politically sophisticated in Europe — it has been managing mortal relations for millennia and is very good at it. Its High King governs through elaborate feudal protocol; its members are aristocratic, grudge-bearing, and patient beyond human comprehension. They do not rage. They wait. They do not conquer. They acquire. What mortals called the Tuatha Dé Danann were not gods but titles — distinctions within a court of immense antiquity. The Sídhe want tribute, the maintenance of old agreements, and children: the changeling tradition reflects a genuine court need, and they have been filling their requirement without mortal consent for as long as the hills have stood. Their bargains are formally stated and deeply consequential — the geis is their signature mechanism, an obligation that seems empowering until it becomes a trap. [See also Lore: Inter-Court Politics — the Sídhe's relationship with the Raven King's Court is the British Isles' defining inter-court tension.]
¹ Mortal euphemisms: Irish — Na Daoine Maithe, Na Daoine Uaisle, Themselves. Scottish — the guid folk, the guid neighbours. Welsh — Tylwyth Teg, Bendith y Mamau. All flattery: calling dangerous beings 'good' is sympathetic magic.
title: The Sídhe Court

keys: The Æsir Court , the court of the Norse gods ,
value:
The Æsir Court. [See also: Norse-Germanic Rune Craft, The One-Eyed Lord, Huginn, Muninn, The Wild Hunt, Sigríður Kolsdóttir, Eiríkr the Walker.]
Accessible through Brocken Peak, Icelandic volcanic sites, and ancient Norse ritual grounds; the court itself is in Faerie, a vast hall of dark timber and firelight that echoes with ravens. The court's lord — referred to in this catalogue by the mortal title 'the One-Eyed Lord,' which is not his name — gave up his eye not because he understood the strategic value of wisdom but because a sacrifice of that shape was, to him, aesthetically beautiful. He hangs from the World Tree for nine nights because such suffering delights him. He sends the Wild Hunt because the sound of hooves on wind pleases him and because terrified mortals sometimes do surprising things when pursued. He collects knowledge the way the Gentleman collects dancers: compulsively, possessively, without concern for cost. He hangs mortals from trees to see if they learn anything interesting. The moment a mortal stops producing something he has not seen before, that mortal ceases to exist for him. What mortals mistook for a philosophy of earned wisdom is a collector's mania applied to suffering-that-produces-insight. Æsir bargains are characterized by brutal directness: the price is stated clearly and it is always terrible. They do not cheat. They do not need to. [See also Lore: Inter-Court Politics — the Æsir's passive-aggressive conflict with the Romuva Court over thunder-sovereignty.]
¹ Mortal euphemisms: Icelandic Huldufólk; Norwegian De Underjordiske; Swedish Vättar. The Wild Hunt leader is never addressed. You hide, or you are taken.
title: The Æsir Court

keys: The Väki Court , the Finnish spirit court ,
value:
The Väki Court. [See also: Finno-Ugric Origin-Song Magic, Tapio, Ahti, Vellamo, The Singing Void, Old Matti Karjalainen.]
The Väki Court has no throne room. Its members are not personalities but forces: fire-väki, water-väki, forest-väki, earth-väki. Its deepest power is the Singing Void — not a being with a form but an origin, a vibration in the depths of Tuonela that contains the true name of everything. Finnish tietäjät who travel far enough to encounter it do not return quite as they were. Dealing with the Väki Court is less like meeting foreign royalty and more like standing in a river and attempting to convince it to change course. The court does not negotiate in any familiar sense: it responds to knowledge, specifically the correct recitation of a thing's origin. Sing the origin-song of fire, and fire-väki cooperates. Sing it incorrectly, and it does not. The price of dealing with the Väki is always epistemic — knowledge of the right kind, acquired completely. Partial knowledge produces partial results, and some origin-songs have been deliberately withheld. Other courts' attempts at diplomatic contact receive, in response, the origin-song of the diplomatic medium employed. The Olympians received the origin-song of sunlight. The Mélusine Court received the origin-song of parchment. Neither court knew what to do with this.
¹ Mortal euphemisms: Finnish noa-names — the bear, most sacred of forest creatures, has over two hundred documented substitute names. Väki forces are addressed as 'the power in the water,' 'the will of the fire.' Direct naming is an invitation for the force to notice you.
title: The Väki Court

keys: The Parliament of Domains , the Slavic spirit parliament ,
value:
The Parliament of Domains. [See also: Slavic Whisper Magic, The Thunder-Voiced, The Cattle-Lord, The Weaver of Fate, Dedushka Petr, The Wandering Domovoi, The Old Man of Białowieża.]
The Parliament convenes on the island of Buyan, where the stone Alatyr sits at the world's centre. Each domain-spirit also maintains individual sovereignty in its own territory — the leshy in each forest, the vodyanoy in each river, the domovoi in each house. There is no single ruler; the great spirits are first among equals, not monarchs. Every domain-spirit has a vote and every domain-spirit has opinions, and the Parliament functions through consensus when it functions at all. Each spirit is, constitutionally, a monomaniac: its domain is its entire world, and the existence of other domains is a polite fiction it tolerates rather than acknowledges. The leshy of Białowieża does not recognize the Parliament's authority; he recognizes only his forest. The domovoi of a single farmhouse considers that farmhouse the centre of creation. Parliament bargains demand strict territorial reciprocity — different spirits want different things in different places, and offending one earns no forgiveness from the others. The return of magic has made the old courtesies urgent: the domovoi in every hearth, the leshy in every wood, are awake and demanding what is owed. [See also Lore: Inter-Court Politics — the Parliament's ongoing border conflict with the Romuva Court.]
¹ Mortal euphemisms: The leshy is 'Forest Grandfather'; the domovoi is 'Grandfather' or simply 'Himself.' All Slavic supernatural beings: Nechistaya sila — 'The Unclean Force,' spoken carefully.
title: The Parliament of Domains

keys: The Romuva Court , the Lithuanian pagan court ,
value:
The Romuva Court. [See also: Baltic Sacred Fire Magic, The Thunderer of the Sacred Oak, Laima, Giltinė, Dalia Kazlauskienė, Vytautas the Flame-Carrier, Žaltvykslė.]
Accessible from the rekindled sacred grove of Samogitia in Lithuania, where the eternal flame has reignited. In Faerie, the Romuva Court is the youngest and the angriest — barely four centuries asleep before magic's return jolted it awake. Its thunder-being, referred to here by the mortal title that also means the common noun for thunder, is not furious in any sense a mortal would recognise as fury. He is proprietary. The sacred groves were his. The prayers were his. He wants them back not because they served any higher purpose but because they were his, and someone took them, and he finds this aesthetically intolerable. His lightning strikes with improving accuracy on church steeples built atop former grove-sites. This is not metaphor. Romuva bargains are conditional loyalty: the court offers weather, fate-knowledge, and household prosperity in exchange for sustained devotion — not a one-time transaction but a permanent commitment that escalates gradually. The danger is obligation creep. A practitioner who began by leaving milk for the sacred grass snake may find the court expects a full grove maintained in perpetuity. [See also Lore: Inter-Court Politics — thunder-sovereignty conflict with the Æsir Court; border conflict with the Parliament.]
¹ Mortal euphemisms: The thunder-being's name is also the word for thunder, making everyday speech a built-in circumlocution. Alternatives: Dundulis ('The Rumbler'), Dievaitis ('Dear God' — deliberately ambiguous). Lithuania, Christianized 1387, has the most recently living pagan naming practice in Europe.
title: The Romuva Court

keys: The Scholomance Court , the Devil's court ,
value:
The Scholomance Court. [See also: Balkan Dragon-Scholar Magic, The Schoolmaster, Gheorghe the Doorkeeper, The Librarian, Andrei Stormwalker, Elena Barbu.]
The Scholomance itself lies underground in the Transylvanian Alps and has reopened as of 1817. Its lord — called 'the Devil' by Romanian peasants and referred to here as the Schoolmaster, neither of which is his name — is the only Fae court leader who actively recruits and educates mortals. He does this not from compassion but from the fascination a naturalist has for an unusual beetle. Mortal learning capacity produces patterns he has not seen before, and new patterns are what he values. His 'pleasure' when a student exceeds expectation is the pleasure of an experiment producing interesting results. He retains one student in ten as permanent faculty — the Weathermaker — not as punishment but because he genuinely does not understand why mortals would object to indefinite retention when the alternative is returning to a world of limited intellectual stimulation. Scholomance bargains are educational contracts: seven years of study, absolute obedience during training, and the tithe risk. Graduates receive genuine power over weather and dragon-riding, and remain bound to the court's service throughout their lives. [See also Lore: Inter-Court Politics — the Weather War between the Scholomance and the Mari Court is the game's primary inter-court conflict; the Balkan partition dispute with the Olympian Court.]
¹ Mortal euphemisms: Romanian Dracul, Naiba; the softer 'Schoolmaster' is this compiler's preferred neutral term. Graduates: Solomonari, Zgrimțieș (Apuseni dialect), Wettermacher (Transylvanian Saxon).
title: The Scholomance Court

keys: The Olympian Court , the court of the Greek gods ,
value:
The Olympian Court. [See also: Mediterranean Eye-Ward Magic, The Lord of the Shining Mountain, The Grey-Eyed Counselor, The Huntress, The Smith, Hermes-Who-Was, Kyriakos Papadimitriou.]
Accessible through caverns beneath the peaks of Olympus — not the summit but underground chambers of white marble. The court's members — the beings mortals remembered as the gods of Greece — are the most nostalgic Fae in Europe. They remember temples, sacrifices, and the organized devotion of entire civilizations, and they have not forgiven the loss. Their lord does not weep for the absence of worship from any sentiment a mortal would recognise as grief. He weeps because the situation is, to him, a considerable aesthetic disappointment. The Olympian Court is proud, brilliant, vain, and petty. Every educated European believes they understand it through classical learning; they are wrong. The myths captured fragments of truth filtered through millennia of distortion.
